items.situation: student asking a professor where to submit a revised essay
items.speaker: Where should I submit the revised essay by Friday?

options.A: The library closes early on Fridays.
options.B: My revisions took me almost all weekend.
options.C: Actually, just email it straight to me.
options.D: No, the deadline hasn't changed.

items.explanation: 学生问教授应该把修改稿交到哪里。正确答案C以话语标记词"Actually"开头，间接地给出投递方式（直接发邮件给我），既回应了"在哪交"的核心需求，又是教授这类权威者自然的口吻。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住"Friday"联想到"图书馆周五早关门"，但完全没说该交到哪里。B是离题项：只谈自己改稿花了多长时间，与"在哪提交"无关，且不是对该问题的回应。D是答错问题类型：用"No"回答了一个关于截止日期是否变动的是非问题，而学生问的是地点（Where），并非deadline是否改变。只有C真正告诉了学生提交的去处。

items.situation: student telling gym staff they want to use the pool
items.speaker: I was hoping to use the pool this afternoon.

options.A: The pool was just repainted this summer.
options.B: I prefer swimming early in the morning.
options.C: Well, the swim team has it until four.
options.D: Yes, the locker rooms are down the hall.

items.explanation: 学生向体育馆工作人员表示下午想用泳池。正确答案C以话语标记词"Well"开头，间接告知现在不行的原因（游泳队四点前占用泳池），这是工作人员对使用请求的自然且得体的回应。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住"pool"去描述泳池被重新粉刷过，并未回应能否使用。B是离题项：只陈述说话者自己"喜欢早上游泳"的个人偏好，放在别的对话里也成立，但没有回应对方的请求。D是答错问题类型/关键词误用：用"Yes"加上更衣室方位来回应，像是在答一个"在哪里"的问题，而学生表达的是下午想用泳池这一意图。只有C真正处理了泳池此刻能否使用。

items.situation: student asking advisor's staff how soon they can get an appointment
items.speaker: How soon can the advisor squeeze me in?

options.A: Her office is just past the stairwell.
options.B: Let me see what I can do.
options.C: Yes, advising is part of her job.
options.D: I already squeezed the oranges this morning.

items.explanation: 学生（向导师办公室的工作人员）询问最快多久能安排上见导师。正确答案B "Let me see what I can do"是一句自然的习惯用语，表示"我去看看能不能安排"，正是工作人员面对加急请求时得体的回应，推进了对话。A是答错问题类型：回答的是导师办公室在哪（Where），而学生问的是"多快"（How soon）。C是离题/泛泛而谈：用"Yes"陈述"提供咨询是她的职责"，听上去相关却没给出任何时间信息，并非对"多久能约上"的回应。D是一词多义陷阱：把"squeeze (someone) in（挤出时间安排某人）"误解成字面的"squeeze oranges（榨橙子）"，完全跑题。只有B回应了能否尽快安排这一核心诉求。

items.situation: student questioning a professor about why an exam section was marked wrong
items.speaker: Why did you mark my whole second section wrong?

options.A: The marker on the whiteboard ran out.
options.B: Section two meets in a different room.
options.C: Office hours are listed on the syllabus.
options.D: Did you address the prompt's actual question?

items.explanation: 学生质问教授为什么把整个第二部分都判错了。正确答案D用一个反问"你回答的是题目真正问的问题吗？"来推进对话，间接点出扣分的可能原因，是教授面对质疑时常见且有效的回应方式。A是语义联想/一词多义陷阱：把动词"mark（批改、判分）"联想成名词"marker（白板笔）"，说笔没墨了，完全跑题。B是语义联想陷阱：抓住"section"联想到"第二部分在另一个教室上课"，与判分对错无关。C是离题项：提到"office hours在教学大纲上"，虽涉及学业但根本没解释为何判错，放在别的对话里才成立。只有D真正回应了"为什么判错"这一核心争议。

items.situation: classmate mentions the gym weight room hours
items.speaker: The coach said the weight room closes early on Fridays.

options.A: I lift weights three times a week.
options.B: The new treadmills arrived this morning.
options.C: Actually, it stays open till nine tonight.
options.D: Yes, the coach is really strict.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用话语标记词"Actually"开头，间接纠正对方的信息——其实今晚开到九点，自然地推进了对话。A是语义联想干扰项（由"weight room"联想到"lift weights"，只谈个人习惯，不回应信息）。B也是语义联想干扰项（由健身房联想到"treadmills"设备，答非所问）。D是错误问题类型/答非所问（用Yes回答了一个陈述句，且只评价教练性格，没回应关门时间）。

items.situation: classmate asking about weekend plans
items.speaker: What are you up to this weekend?

options.A: Well, I'm just catching up on sleep.
options.B: The weekend forecast looks pretty rainy.
options.C: Yes, I had a great time.
options.D: It's up on the second floor.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用话语标记词"Well"开头，间接而自然地回答了周末打算干什么——补觉，符合口语习惯。B是语义联想干扰项（由"weekend"联想到"weekend forecast"天气，偏离了"你打算干什么"）。C是错误问题类型（用Yes回答了一个本应说明计划的WH问句，且谈的是过去而非这个周末）。D是一词多义/答非所问陷阱（把"up to"误当成方位"up on"，回答了位置）。

items.situation: student asking a librarian where to return reserved books
items.speaker: Where do I return the reserved books?

options.A: I reserved them online last night.
options.B: Those books are due next Tuesday.
options.C: Just drop them at the front desk.
options.D: Yes, you can borrow up to five.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用话语标记词"Just"开头，间接而简洁地指明了归还地点——前台，直接解决了对方的需求。A是语义联想干扰项（由"reserved"联想到"reserved them online"，谈的是怎么预约的，不是去哪还）。B也是语义联想干扰项（由"books"联想到还书"due date"，答非所问）。D是错误问题类型（用Yes回答了一个问地点的Where问句，且谈的是借阅上限）。

items.situation: two classmates deciding where to get lunch
items.speaker: I'm starving, but the dining hall's packed.

options.A: The dining hall serves pasta on Mondays.
options.B: Yes, I ate lunch an hour ago.
options.C: It's right next to the library.
options.D: Want to try the cafe instead?

items.explanation: 正确答案D用一个反问"Want to try the cafe instead?"推进对话，提出去咖啡馆这一替代方案，直接化解了"食堂太挤"的困境。A是语义联想干扰项（由"dining hall"联想到食堂供应的食物，答非所问）。B是答非所问/错误语境（用Yes回应，且只谈自己一小时前吃过，没帮对方解决饿和拥挤的问题）。C是错误问题类型（回答了"在哪儿"这个位置问题，而对方根本没问食堂在哪）。

items.situation: student asking a professor how to approach an upcoming exam
items.speaker: How should I prepare for the final exam?

options.A: Just review the lecture notes thoroughly.
options.B: The exam is in the main hall.
options.C: I prepared a snack for the trip.
options.D: Yes, you can bring a calculator.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用'Just'这一话语标记词引出一个直接而有用的备考建议，自然回应了'怎么准备'的问题。B是语义联想干扰项：抓住'exam'联想到考场地点，答非所问（问的是怎么准备，不是在哪考）。C是离题干扰项：把'prepare'误用到'准备零食'上，与备考毫无关系。D是错误问题类型干扰项：用yes/no回答能否带计算器，而问题问的是'如何'(how)准备。

items.situation: advisor letting a student know about a schedule change
items.speaker: I moved our meeting to Thursday afternoon.

options.A: The meeting room is quite large.
options.B: Actually, that works better for me.
options.C: I moved into my dorm last week.
options.D: No, I haven't met her yet.

items.explanation: 正确答案B以话语标记词'Actually'开头，间接表达'那个时间反而更合适'，自然接受了改期。A是语义联想干扰项：抓住'meeting'联想到会议室大小，没有回应改期。C是语义联想干扰项：复用动词'moved'但说成'搬进宿舍'，与改约会面无关。D是离题干扰项：用'no'回答'见没见过她'，与本对话语境完全不符。

items.situation: student asking a classmate what enrolling in an advanced course requires
items.speaker: What do I need to take the advanced seminar?

options.A: The seminar room is on the fourth floor.
options.B: I took that class last spring.
options.C: You've got to finish the intro course first.
options.D: When does the seminar usually meet?

items.explanation: 正确答案C用口语习惯表达'You've got to...'(你得先...)指出选修高级研讨课的前提是先修完入门课，直接而地道地回应了'需要什么'。A是语义联想干扰项：抓住'seminar'联想到教室楼层，答非所问。B是错误问题类型/离题干扰项：复用动词'took'谈自己上过那门课，没有回答需要什么前提。D是错误问题类型干扰项：用一个问'什么时候上课'(when)的反问，偏离了'需要什么前提'(what)的问题。

items.situation: student checking the front desk for a lost item
items.speaker: Did anyone turn in a blue umbrella?

options.A: The umbrella stand is by the door.
options.B: Yes, I turned in my assignment.
options.C: Where did you buy the umbrella?
options.D: Let me check the lost-and-found box.

items.explanation: 正确答案D以'Let me...'引出实际行动——去失物招领箱查看，间接而恰当地回应了寻物请求。A是语义联想干扰项：抓住'umbrella'联想到伞架位置，没有去查找。B是语义联想/错误问题类型干扰项：复用动词'turn in'说成'交了作业'，与捡到的伞无关。C是错误问题类型干扰项：用一个问'在哪买的'的反问，偏离了'有没有人上交'这个yes/no问题。

items.situation: friend asking why another turned down a weekend invitation
items.speaker: Why can't you come to the party?

options.A: I'll stop by after my shift ends.
options.B: The party starts at eight.
options.C: Because the music was too loud.
options.D: Yes, I had a great time there.

items.explanation: 正确答案A没有直接解释原因，而是转向解决根本问题——'下班后我会顺道过来'，化解了对方的失望，是典型的语境转移。B是语义联想干扰项：抓住'party'联想到派对开始时间，没有回应为什么不能来。C是误解/离题干扰项：用'because'看似回答原因，却说'音乐太吵'且时态用过去式，与未能赴约无关。D是错误问题类型干扰项：用yes/no回答'玩得开不开心'，偏离了'为什么不能来'(why)的问题。

items.situation: friend inviting a classmate to join a study group
items.speaker: Do you want to join our study group?

options.A: The group project is due Monday.
options.B: Count me in.
options.C: I joined the gym yesterday.
options.D: Where is the library entrance?

items.explanation: 正确答案B用地道口语习惯表达'Count me in.'(算我一个)爽快地接受了邀请。A是语义联想干扰项：抓住'group'联想到小组作业截止日期，没有回应邀请。C是语义联想干扰项：复用动词'joined'说成'加入了健身房'，与学习小组无关。D是错误问题类型干扰项：用一个问'图书馆入口在哪'(where)的反问，偏离了'要不要加入'这个yes/no问题。

items.situation: two friends deciding where to grab lunch
items.speaker: The cafe by the gym has great sandwiches.

options.A: The gym closes at ten tonight.
options.B: I made a sandwich this morning.
options.C: Want to head there now?
options.D: Yes, I exercise three times a week.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用一个推进对话的反问'要不要现在过去？'回应了对方的推荐，自然引向下一步行动。A是语义联想干扰项：抓住'gym'联想到健身房关门时间，没有回应吃饭提议。B是语义联想干扰项：复用'sandwich'说成'早上做了三明治'，与去那家咖啡馆吃饭无关。D是错误问题类型/离题干扰项：用yes/no回答自己锻炼频率，偏离了关于午餐地点的话题。

items.situation: student asking staff where to find the gym's hours
items.speaker: Where can I find the gym's hours?

options.A: The gym has brand-new treadmills.
options.B: I go running every morning.
options.C: Yes, the gym is open today.
options.D: They're posted on the front door.

items.explanation: 正确答案D直接而恰当地告知开放时间张贴在哪里——'贴在前门上'，间接回应了'在哪能找到'的问题。A是语义联想干扰项：抓住'gym'联想到崭新的跑步机，没有告知在哪查时间。B是离题干扰项：说自己每天晨跑，与查询开放时间无关。C是错误问题类型干扰项：用yes/no回答'今天开不开'，偏离了'在哪能找到时间'(where)的问题。

items.situation: student asking a professor for feedback after submitting a draft
items.speaker: What did you think of my essay draft?

options.A: Well, your argument needs a clearer thesis.
options.B: The essay contest closes next week.
options.C: I drafted three players for my team.
options.D: Did you print it double-sided?

items.explanation: 正确答案A以话语标记词'Well'开头，间接给出对草稿的评价——'你的论点需要更清晰的论题'，自然回应了请求反馈。B是语义联想干扰项：抓住'essay'联想到征文比赛截止日期，没有给出评价。C是一词多义干扰项：复用'draft'但用作'选秀/挑选球员'之意，与文章草稿无关。D是错误问题类型干扰项：用一个问'是否双面打印'的yes/no反问，偏离了'觉得我的草稿怎么样'(what)的问题。

items.situation: librarian telling a student a requested book has arrived
items.speaker: Your reserved book just came in.

options.A: The library closes at midnight.
options.B: Great, I'll pick it up today.
options.C: I booked a flight for the break.
options.D: No, I haven't read it yet.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用'Great'回应好消息，并接着说'我今天就来取'，自然而恰当。A是语义联想干扰项：抓住'book/library'联想到图书馆闭馆时间，没有回应取书。C是一词多义干扰项：复用'book'但用作动词'预订（机票）'，与预约的书无关。D是离题干扰项：用'no'回答'读没读过'，与'书到了'这一通知不对应。

items.situation: resident assistant asking a student about a noise complaint
items.speaker: Did you hear loud music last night?

options.A: Yes, the speakers were on sale.
options.B: Music theory is my favorite class.
options.C: Was it coming from the next room?
options.D: The hallway lights were off.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用一个推进对话的反问'是从隔壁房间传来的吗？'回应了询问，既确认听到又追问来源。A是语义联想/离题干扰项：复用概念用'yes'后却说'音箱在打折'，答非所问。B是语义联想干扰项：抓住'music'联想到乐理课，与昨晚的噪音无关。D是离题干扰项：说'走廊灯关着'，与是否听到吵闹音乐无关。

items.situation: coach reminding an athlete about practice timing
items.speaker: Practice starts an hour earlier tomorrow.

options.A: The field is across from the gym.
options.B: Okay, I'll set my alarm earlier.
options.C: I practiced the piano all weekend.
options.D: No, I didn't win the match.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用'Okay'接受提醒，并说'那我把闹钟调早'，自然回应了训练提前的通知。A是语义联想干扰项：抓住'practice'联想到训练场位置，没有回应时间变动。C是一词多义/语义联想干扰项：复用'practice'但用作'练琴'，与球队训练无关。D是离题干扰项：用'no'回答'有没有赢比赛'，与训练提前一小时不对应。

items.situation: student asking staff where to pick up a parking permit
items.speaker: Where do I pick up my parking permit?

options.A: Parking on campus is pretty expensive.
options.B: Did you renew it online?
options.C: I parked near the stadium today.
options.D: Just stop by the security office.

items.explanation: 正确答案D以'Just'引出地点——'去趟保安办公室就行'，直接而恰当地回应了'在哪领'的问题。A是语义联想干扰项：抓住'parking'联想到停车很贵，答非所问。B是错误问题类型干扰项：用一个问'是否网上续办'的yes/no反问，偏离了'在哪领'(where)的问题。C是语义联想干扰项：复用动词'parked'说成'今天停在体育馆附近'，与领许可证无关。

items.situation: classmate asking another how a tough midterm went
items.speaker: How did the chemistry midterm go?

options.A: It was a piece of cake.
options.B: The chemistry lab is downstairs.
options.C: I bought the textbook yesterday.
options.D: Yes, the professor is very kind.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用地道习语'a piece of cake'(小菜一碟)回答考得怎么样，地道而自然。B是语义联想干扰项：抓住'chemistry'联想到化学实验室位置，没有回应考得如何。C是语义联想干扰项：说昨天买了课本，与这次期中考表现无关。D是错误问题类型干扰项：用yes/no回答'教授是否友善'，偏离了'考得怎么样'(how)的问题。

items.situation: staff member asking a student to confirm a registration detail
items.speaker: Can you verify your student ID number?

options.A: It's printed on the back of my card.
options.B: The ID office is down the hall.
options.C: Yes, I verified the results twice.
options.D: I lost my wallet last weekend.

items.explanation: 正确答案A没有直接报出号码，而是转向解决问题——'它印在我卡片背面'，让对方自行核对，是巧妙的语境转移。B是语义联想干扰项：抓住'ID'联想到学籍办公室位置，答非所问。C是一词多义干扰项：复用动词'verify'但用作'核对结果'，与核实学号无关。D是离题干扰项：说上周丢了钱包，与核实学号不对应。

items.situation: professor asking a student to present findings sooner than planned
items.speaker: Could you present your findings next week?

options.A: The findings were quite surprising.
options.B: I presented at the fair last year.
options.C: Would Wednesday give me enough time?
options.D: Yes, the conference room seats forty.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用一个推进对话的反问'周三能给我足够时间吗？'回应了请求，既不拒绝又商讨可行性。A是语义联想干扰项：抓住'findings'联想到'结果很出人意料'，没有回应能否下周汇报。B是离题/错误问题类型干扰项：复用动词'presented'谈去年在展会上汇报，与下周的安排无关。D是错误问题类型干扰项：用yes/no后却说'会议室能坐四十人'，偏离了能否下周汇报这一时间安排问题。

items.situation: classmate asking what to bring to a group ride
items.speaker: What time should I be ready for the carpool?

options.A: Be downstairs by eight, okay?
options.B: The car needs an oil change soon.
options.C: I always carpool to save gas.
options.D: Where did you park the van?

items.explanation: 正确答案A直接回答了"几点准备好"这个时间问题，给出明确时间"八点下楼"，符合direct_topical范式。B是语义联想陷阱，听到"carpool"联想到"car"和"oil change"，描述车辆保养，与时间无关。C重复使用"carpool"一词但只是陈述个人习惯，没有回答时间。D答非所问：对方问"几点"（时间），D却问"在哪里停车"（地点），属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: roommate proposing a weekend trip
items.speaker: How does a beach trip this Saturday sound?

options.A: It sounds pretty far from here.
options.B: Count me in!
options.C: The speakers sound a bit broken.
options.D: I went there last summer.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用习语"Count me in!"（算我一个）表示欣然接受邀请，符合idiomatic范式，是对"听起来怎么样"最自然的口语回应。A误解了"sound"，把它当作"听起来遥远"，偏离了征询意见的本意。C是多义词陷阱，把"sound"（听起来）误用成"声音"，说音响坏了，完全偏题。D虽与海滩相关，但只是陈述去年去过，没有回应这次的邀请，属于偏题。

items.situation: friend announcing they got a new job
items.speaker: Where do you think I just got hired?

options.A: I got fired once too.
options.B: The office is downtown.
options.C: No way, tell me everything!
options.D: Hiring season is in spring.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用口语习语"No way, tell me everything!"（不会吧，快全告诉我！）表达惊喜并鼓励对方分享好消息，符合idiomatic范式，是分享喜讯时最自然的回应。A是语义联想陷阱，听到"hired"联想到反义词"fired"，且把话题转到自己身上。B答非所问：对方其实是在兴奋地分享消息，而非真的问地点，B却字面回答了办公室位置。D重复"hiring"一词但只陈述招聘季节的常识，与对方的喜讯无关，属于偏题。

items.situation: two friends deciding where to get lunch
items.speaker: That new noodle place just opened near the library.

options.A: I read a great book there.
options.B: Noodles take forever to cook.
options.C: The library closes at nine tonight.
options.D: Let's try it for lunch today.

items.explanation: 正确答案D直接回应了同伴的潜台词——推荐去新面馆吃饭，提议"今天去那儿吃午饭"，符合direct_topical范式，自然推进了关于午餐的对话。A是语义联想陷阱，听到"library"联想到"book"，说在那读了本好书，偏离吃饭话题。B重复"noodles"一词但只抱怨煮面慢，没有回应一起去吃的提议。C是语义联想陷阱，抓住"library"说图书馆几点关门，与午餐无关。

items.situation: checking if the post office is open
items.speaker: I really hope the post office is still open.

options.A: Actually, they close at five today.
options.B: I love sending postcards.
options.C: The post office hired new staff.
options.D: Did you mail the package yet?

items.explanation: 正确答案A以话语标记"Actually"开头，间接回应对方的担忧，提供关键信息"今天五点关门"，符合marker_led_indirect范式。B是语义联想陷阱，听到"post office"联想到"postcards"，只表达个人爱好，没回应能否赶上。C重复"post office"一词但说招了新员工，与是否还开门无关。D答非所问：对方担心的是邮局是否还开着，D却反问包裹寄了没，转移了焦点，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: friend inviting another to a study group
items.speaker: Why don't you join our study session tonight?

options.A: The session lasted three hours.
options.B: You know what, I just might.
options.C: I don't know why it failed.
options.D: Studying alone works for me.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用口语习语"You know what, I just might."（你别说，我还真可能去）以话语标记开头，表示被说动而欣然考虑加入，符合idiomatic范式。注意"Why don't you..."是邀请而非询问原因。A是语义联想陷阱，重复"session"一词，只陈述上次时长，没回应邀请。C误把"Why"当成真的问原因，答"不知道为什么失败了"，属于误解意图。D虽相关，却是间接拒绝（独自学习更适合我），但题目要求选"接受"的回应，D偏离了被邀请后顺势加入的自然走向，属于偏题干扰。

items.situation: student asking an RA about a dorm heating problem
items.speaker: Can you get someone to fix our heater today?

options.A: The heater is made of metal.
options.B: Heat rises to the top floor.
options.C: I'll get right on it.
options.D: Did you turn the thermostat up?

items.explanation: 正确答案C用口语习语"I'll get right on it."（我这就去办）表示立即着手解决报修请求，符合idiomatic范式，是宿管面对维修请求最得体的回应。A是语义联想陷阱，听到"heater"联想其材质，说暖气是金属做的，毫无帮助。B是语义联想陷阱，抓住"heat"说热气上升到顶楼，偏离修理请求。D答非所问：对方请求的是"找人来修"，D却反问是否调高了温控器，转成另一个问题，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: student asking a professor about an assignment deadline
items.speaker: Did you extend the deadline for the lab report?

options.A: The report needs three sections.
options.B: Labs are held on Tuesdays.
options.C: Yes, I wrote it last night.
options.D: Well, you now have until Friday.

items.explanation: 正确答案D以话语标记"Well"开头，间接确认延期并给出新截止日"现在到周五"，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A是语义联想陷阱，听到"report"联想其结构，说报告需要三个部分，没回答是否延期。B是语义联想陷阱，抓住"lab"说实验课在周二，与截止日无关。C看似回答"Yes"，但"我昨晚写完了"是学生口吻，且把"extend"误当成自己写报告，对教授而言语境完全错误，属于错误语境干扰。

items.situation: professor checking whether a student finished the reading
items.speaker: Have you finished the chapter for today?

options.A: The chapter had twenty pages.
options.B: Chapters are listed in the syllabus.
options.C: Honestly, I'm only halfway through.
options.D: Did we have a reading due?

items.explanation: 正确答案C以话语标记"Honestly"开头，坦诚地间接回应"只读了一半"，符合marker_led_indirect范式，是学生面对教授询问时自然得体的回答。A是语义联想陷阱，听到"chapter"联想其篇幅，说有二十页，没回答读完没。B是语义联想陷阱，抓住"chapter"说大纲里列了章节，偏离问题。D答非所问：教授问的是"读完没"，D却反问"今天有阅读任务吗"，把已知前提变成疑问，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: advisor asking a student about course registration
items.speaker: Which electives are you planning to take?

options.A: Electives don't count toward the major.
options.B: What would you recommend for me?
options.C: I elected the class president.
options.D: The lecture hall seats two hundred.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用反问"您会推荐我选什么呢？"将问题抛回给顾问，借助其专业意见推进对话，符合counter_question范式，是面对选课咨询时自然的互动。A是语义联想陷阱，重复"electives"一词，只陈述选修不算专业学分的事实，没说自己打算选什么。C是多义词陷阱，把"elective"（选修课）与动词"elect"（选举）混淆，说选了班长，完全偏题。D是偏题干扰，说讲堂能坐两百人，与选哪些选修课无关。

items.situation: librarian telling a student a book is overdue
items.speaker: This book was actually due back last week.

options.A: Oh no, I completely lost track.
options.B: The book has a torn cover.
options.C: I love reading mystery novels.
options.D: Where is the returns desk located?

items.explanation: 正确答案A以感叹"Oh no"开头，间接承认自己忘了还期"完全没记着"，符合marker_led_indirect范式，是被告知逾期时自然的口语反应。B是语义联想陷阱，听到"book"联想其外观，说封面破了，与逾期无关。C是语义联想陷阱，抓住"book"说爱读悬疑小说，偏离话题。D答非所问：图书管理员指出的是"已逾期"这一事实，D却反问还书台在哪，回避了逾期问题，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: coach telling a player about a schedule change
items.speaker: We're moving practice to six in the morning.

options.A: Morning people are more productive.
options.B: Should I bring my own water then?
options.C: The practice field was just repainted.
options.D: I practiced piano this morning.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用反问"那我要不要自己带水？"接受了改时间的安排并顺势询问准备事项，符合counter_question范式，自然推进对话。A是偏题干扰，泛泛地说早起的人更高效，没回应训练改期。C是语义联想陷阱，重复"practice"一词，说训练场刚重新划线，与时间变动无关。D是语义联想陷阱，抓住"morning"和"practice"说自己早上练了钢琴，把"practice"误用成另一种语境，偏离了球队训练话题。

items.situation: staff member explaining a closed building
items.speaker: The student center's closed for renovations.

options.A: So where should I go instead?
options.B: The center has a nice fountain.
options.C: Students love studying there.
options.D: I renovated my room last month.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用反问"那我该去哪儿呢？"在得知学生中心关闭后顺势询问替代方案，符合counter_question范式，自然推进对话。B是语义联想陷阱，听到"center"联想其设施，说有个漂亮喷泉，与关闭无关。C是语义联想陷阱，抓住地点说学生爱在那学习，没回应关闭的影响。D是语义联想陷阱，重复"renovate"一词但说自己上月翻新了房间，把话题转到自己身上，偏离了学生中心关闭这一信息。

items.situation: classmate asking about a missed lecture
items.speaker: What did I miss in today's lecture?

options.A: I'll send you my notes tonight.
options.B: The lecture ran ten minutes over.
options.C: I missed the bus this morning.
options.D: When does the next lecture start?

items.explanation: 正确答案A没有逐条复述讲了什么，而是直接解决对方的根本需求——"今晚把我的笔记发给你"，符合context_shift范式，比口头转述更实用。B是语义联想陷阱，重复"lecture"一词，只说课拖堂十分钟，没回应错过的内容。C是语义联想陷阱，抓住"miss"说自己早上错过了公交，把"miss"误用成另一语境，偏离话题。D答非所问：对方问"错过了什么"，D却反问"下节课几点开始"，转成时间问题，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: advisor reminding a student about a missing form
items.speaker: Your enrollment form is still missing a signature.

options.A: My bad, let me sign it now.
options.B: The form is printed on blue paper.
options.C: Signatures are hard to forge.
options.D: How many students enrolled this year?

items.explanation: 正确答案A用口语习语"My bad"（是我疏忽）承认遗漏并立即补签，符合idiomatic范式，是被指出表格缺签名时自然得体的回应。B是语义联想陷阱，听到"form"联想其外观，说表格印在蓝纸上，没回应缺签名。C是语义联想陷阱，抓住"signature"说签名难伪造，偏离话题。D答非所问：顾问指出的是"缺签名"，D却反问今年多少学生注册，转成统计问题，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: staff member at the front desk asking for ID
items.speaker: Could I see your student ID for a second?

options.A: The ID photo looks nothing like me.
options.B: Students get a discount here.
options.C: I saw the new ID design online.
options.D: Sure, just give me a moment.

items.explanation: 正确答案D以"Sure"开头，配合"稍等一下"间接而配合地回应出示证件的请求，符合marker_led_indirect范式，是被要求出示学生证时自然得体的回应。A是语义联想陷阱，听到"ID"联想到照片，吐槽照片不像本人，没有配合出示。B是语义联想陷阱，抓住"student"说这里学生有折扣，偏离请求。C是语义联想陷阱，重复"ID"一词，说在网上看过新证件设计，与出示证件无关。

items.situation: student asking RA about the dorm laundry room hours
items.speaker: The laundry room never seems to be open late.

options.A: Actually, it's open until midnight now.
options.B: I prefer washing on weekends.
options.C: The dryer broke down again.
options.D: She moved to a new dorm.

items.explanation: 说话者抱怨洗衣房似乎很晚就不开了，正确答案A直接给出事实信息（现在开到午夜），纠正了对方的误解，是直接的话题回应。B答非所问，只谈个人偏好（周末洗衣），没有回应开放时间；C是语义联想陷阱，借用'laundry room'联想到'dryer'(烘干机)，但只描述烘干机坏了，未回应营业时间；D完全跑题，谈论某人搬到新宿舍，与洗衣房时间无关。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report formatting
items.speaker: Why do we have to submit the lab report twice?

options.A: The lab is on the second floor.
options.B: Didn't you read the syllabus update?
options.C: Reports are usually graded fairly.
options.D: I submitted mine last night.

items.explanation: 说话者问为什么实验报告要提交两次，正确答案B用反问推进对话（你没看课程大纲的更新吗？），暗示答案就在大纲里，引导对方自己去查，是典型的反问式回应。A是语义联想陷阱，借'lab'联想到实验室位置（二楼），答非所问；C泛泛地说报告评分公平，与'为什么提交两次'无关，属跑题；D只说自己昨晚交了报告，回应的是'你交了吗'而非'为什么交两次'，属错误问题类型。

items.situation: student asking a professor about an assignment extension
items.speaker: Did you get the email I sent about my essay?

options.A: Email is faster than regular mail.
options.B: Yes, I check my inbox daily.
options.C: Take two extra days if you need them.
options.D: No, essays are due on Friday.

items.explanation: 说话者问教授是否收到关于论文的邮件，真实意图是想申请延期。正确答案C不直接回答是否收到邮件，而是直接解决潜在需求（需要的话多给你两天），属于语境转移式回应。A是语义联想陷阱，借'email'谈邮件比普通邮寄快，完全没回应学生；B看似回答'是'，但只说自己每天查收件箱，是常见的对话开场却不解决问题，属于在'Do you...'问题里答YES+无用信息的歧义陷阱式跑题；D答的是'论文什么时候交'而非'你收到邮件了吗'，属错误问题类型。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend who seems stressed
items.speaker: How are you holding up with all those exams?

options.A: The exam hall was really crowded.
options.B: I'll hold the door for you.
options.C: Yes, that's the right room.
options.D: Honestly, I'm pretty exhausted.

items.explanation: 说话者关心朋友在考试压力下过得怎么样，正确答案D直接坦诚地回答自己的状态（说实话，我挺累的），是直接的话题回应。A是语义联想陷阱，借'exams'联想到考场拥挤，描述环境却不回应'你怎么样'；B是多义/误解陷阱，把'holding up'(撑住、应对)误解成'hold'(扶住门)，答非所问；C回答的是'这是不是对的房间'这类是非问，与'你过得怎么样'毫不相干，属错误问题类型。

items.situation: calling a clinic to check its hours
items.speaker: What time does the clinic close on Fridays?

options.A: We can squeeze you in this afternoon.
options.B: The waiting room has free coffee.
options.C: Fridays are usually our busiest day.
options.D: It's right next to the pharmacy.

items.explanation: 表面上问诊所周五几点关门，真实意图是想尽快预约看病。正确答案A不报关门时间，而是直接解决潜在需求（今天下午能给你挤个号），属语境转移式回应。B是语义联想陷阱，借'clinic'联想到候诊室有免费咖啡，描述设施却不回应；C泛泛说周五最忙，看似相关却不给具体时间也不解决预约需求，属跑题；D答的是诊所'在哪里'（药房旁边），回应的是地点问题而非'几点关门'，属错误问题类型。

items.situation: student telling a classmate the shuttle is late again
items.speaker: The campus shuttle is running late again.

options.A: The driver waved at me earlier.
options.B: Tell me about it.
options.C: I caught the late shuttle home.
options.D: Buses can hold forty people.

items.explanation: 同学抱怨校园班车又晚点了，正确答案B用习语'Tell me about it'(可不是嘛/我太有同感了)表示强烈认同，是自然的口语化回应。A是语义联想陷阱，借'shuttle'联想到司机，谈司机向我挥手，与晚点无关；C是单词陷阱，重复使用'late shuttle'但讲的是自己坐晚班车回家这件无关的过去事件；D完全跑题，陈述公交车能坐四十人这一事实，与晚点无关。

items.situation: acknowledging a friend cannot make it to a planned meetup
items.speaker: Are you sure you can't make it tonight?

options.A: Yes, I made it myself.
options.B: The restaurant is fully booked.
options.C: Well, maybe I can stop by briefly.
options.D: No, I haven't met him yet.

items.explanation: 说话者确认朋友今晚是否真的来不了，正确答案C用话语标记'Well'开头，给出间接回应（也许我能短暂过来一下），既软化又推进对话，属标记引导的间接回应。A是多义/误解陷阱，把'make it'(到场)误解成'made it myself'(亲手做的)，答非所问；B是语义联想陷阱，借聚会场景联想到餐厅订满，描述无关情况；D把'make it'误读成'meet him'(见过他)，回答的是'你见过他了吗'，属错误问题类型。

items.situation: student checking the lost-and-found desk for a misplaced item
items.speaker: Where do I report a lost student ID?

options.A: I lost mine last semester too.
options.B: The ID photo looks just like you.
options.C: Yes, lots of things get turned in here.
options.D: Actually, the front desk handles that.

items.explanation: 说话者问在哪里挂失学生证，正确答案D用话语标记'Actually'开头，间接但明确地指出处理地点（其实前台负责这个），属标记引导的间接回应。A是歧义型干扰项的反例改造——这里改为只谈自己上学期也丢过却不给地点，属跑题且不提供有用信息；B是语义联想陷阱，借'ID'联想到证件照片像本人，描述无关细节；C回答的是是非式开场（是的，很多东西交到这里）却不回应'在哪里挂失'这一地点问题，属错误问题类型。

items.situation: classmate asking which sources count for the research paper
items.speaker: Do blog posts count as valid sources?

options.A: I posted on the class forum yesterday.
options.B: The library opens at eight.
options.C: Have you asked the professor about that?
options.D: Yes, I write a blog myself.

items.explanation: 同学问博客文章能否算作有效的引用来源，正确答案C用反问推进对话（你问过教授这事吗？），把问题引向权威解答，属反问式回应。A是语义联想陷阱，借'posts'联想到自己在课程论坛发帖，答非所问；B完全跑题，谈图书馆八点开门，与来源是否有效无关；D是歧义陷阱的反例改造——把'blog'重复使用并答'是的，我自己写博客'，回应的是'你写博客吗'而非'博客算不算来源'，属错误问题类型。

items.situation: student asking the librarian how to find a reserved book
items.speaker: How do I find a book on course reserve?

options.A: Just ask at the desk behind you.
options.B: Books on that shelf are for sale.
options.C: Yes, you can borrow up to ten.
options.D: The course starts next Monday.

items.explanation: 学生问怎么找课程指定参考书，正确答案A用话语标记'Just'开头给出简洁的间接指引（在你身后的台子问一下就行），属标记引导的间接回应。B是语义联想陷阱，借'book'联想到那个书架上的书在出售，描述无关情况；C回答的是是非式问题（是的，你最多能借十本），回应的是借阅数量而非'怎么找'，属错误问题类型；D是单词陷阱，借'course'联想到课程下周一开始，与找书无关。

items.situation: advisor checking whether a student declared a major
items.speaker: Have you decided on a major yet?

options.A: The major requirements are posted online.
options.B: Let's go over your options together.
options.C: Yes, the deadline was last week.
options.D: No, I major in the morning classes.

items.explanation: 顾问问学生是否已决定专业，学生的潜在需求是拿不定主意。正确答案B不直接回答是否决定，而是解决潜在困难（我们一起来过一遍你的选择），属语境转移式回应。A是语义联想陷阱，借'major'联想到专业要求贴在网上，答非所问；C回答的是'截止日期'相关信息而非'你定了吗'，属错误问题类型；D是多义/误解陷阱，把'major'(专业)误用成动词搭配'major in the morning classes'，逻辑荒谬却语法尚通，属误解。

items.situation: coach telling a player about a schedule change for practice
items.speaker: We're switching practice to Thursday mornings.

options.A: Does that mean Tuesdays are free now?
options.B: The field gets muddy after rain.
options.C: I practice the piano every morning.
options.D: Yes, mornings are colder lately.

items.explanation: 教练告知把训练改到周四上午，正确答案A用反问推进对话（那是不是周二就空出来了？），自然地确认日程变化的影响，属反问式回应。B是语义联想陷阱，借'practice'联想到训练场雨后泥泞，描述无关情况；C是单词陷阱，重复'practice'但讲的是练钢琴这件无关的事；D只附和'是的，最近上午更冷了'，回应的是天气而非日程变更，属跑题。

items.situation: staff member at the housing office explaining a form
items.speaker: Can you fill out this housing form by Friday?

options.A: The housing office is upstairs.
options.B: Yes, forms are usually two pages.
options.C: Could you walk me through it first?
options.D: No, I filled the tank this morning.

items.explanation: 工作人员让学生周五前填好住宿表，学生的潜在困难是不会填。正确答案C不简单答能或不能，而是解决潜在障碍（你能先带我过一遍吗？），属语境转移式回应。A是语义联想陷阱，借'housing'联想到住宿办公室在楼上，答非所问；B看似答'是'却只说表格通常两页，是'Can you...'问题里答YES+无用信息的歧义跑题；D是多义/误解陷阱，把'fill out'(填表)误解成'filled the tank'(加满油箱)，答非所问。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend who missed several classes
items.speaker: How come you weren't in class all week?

options.A: The classroom was on the fourth floor.
options.B: Yes, the week went by fast.
options.C: I sit in the front row usually.
options.D: I caught a nasty cold.

items.explanation: 说话者关心朋友为什么一整周没来上课，正确答案D直接给出原因（我得了重感冒），是直接的话题回应。A是语义联想陷阱，借'class'联想到教室在四楼，描述地点却不回应原因；B只附和'是的，这周过得真快'，回应的是时间感受而非'为什么没来'，属跑题；C是语义联想陷阱，借'class'联想到自己平时坐前排，与缺课原因无关。

items.situation: calling a bookstore to ask about its weekend hours
items.speaker: What are your hours this weekend?

options.A: We can hold the book for you till Monday.
options.B: Our staff is very friendly here.
options.C: Weekends are great for reading.
options.D: The store is on Maple Street.

items.explanation: 表面问书店周末营业时间，真实意图常是担心赶不上来取某本书。正确答案A不报时间，而是直接解决潜在需求（我们可以帮你把书留到周一），属语境转移式回应。B是语义联想陷阱，借'店'联想到员工友好，描述无关印象；C泛泛说周末适合读书，与营业时间无关，属跑题；D答的是书店'在哪条街'，回应的是地点而非'营业时间'，属错误问题类型。

items.situation: student asking the front desk where to claim a found umbrella
items.speaker: Where can I claim a lost umbrella?

options.A: Well, check the bin by the entrance.
options.B: It's been raining all morning.
options.C: Yes, plenty of umbrellas pile up here.
options.D: The forecast says clear skies tomorrow.

items.explanation: 学生问在哪里认领丢失的雨伞，正确答案A用话语标记'Well'开头给出间接指引（去入口旁边那个箱子看看），属标记引导的间接回应。B是语义联想陷阱，借'umbrella'联想到下了一上午雨，描述天气却不回应地点；C回答的是是非式开场（是的，这里堆了很多伞）却不指明'在哪里认领'，属错误问题类型；D是语义联想陷阱，借'umbrella/rain'联想到明天天气预报晴，与认领地点无关。

items.situation: responding to an invitation or suggestion
items.speaker: How am I supposed to get to your place?

options.A: Don't worry, I'll come pick you up.
options.B: My place is bigger than yours.
options.C: I got there really late last night.
options.D: Yes, you should definitely come along.

items.explanation: 说话人表面问"怎么去你家"，实际担心交通方式。正确答案A不直接回答方式，而是直接解决他的难题——主动开车去接，属于context_shift。B是语义关联陷阱：抓住"place"却去比较房子大小，答非所问。C是语义关联陷阱：复用"get there"但说的是昨晚到达，与当前安排无关。D是错误问题类型：回答的是"我该不该来"这种是非问，而非"怎么来"。

items.situation: student discussing a presentation topic with partner
items.speaker: Do you think our topic is too broad?

options.A: Yes, the room is pretty broad.
options.B: Actually, we could narrow it down.
options.C: The presentation is on Tuesday.
options.D: I gave a great talk last semester.

items.explanation: 说话人问选题是否太宽泛。正确答案B以话语标记"Actually"开头，间接回应——承认问题并提出缩小范围，属于marker_led_indirect。A是语义关联陷阱兼一词多义：把"broad"（宽泛）误用为"宽敞"形容房间。C是离题选项：说的是展示时间，与选题宽窄无关。D是离题选项：谈论上学期自己的演讲，与当前选题无关。

items.situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed
items.speaker: You look like you've got a lot on your plate.

options.A: The plate is on the kitchen table.
options.B: I ordered way too much food.
options.C: Yeah, let me know if you can help out.
options.D: She looks really tired today.

items.explanation: 说话人用习语"a lot on your plate"表示"事情很多/压力大"，是在表达关心。正确答案C顺势回应——承认并请求帮助，自然推进对话。A是语义关联陷阱兼一词多义：把"plate"字面理解为餐盘。B是语义关联陷阱：抓住"plate"联想到点餐过多，答非所问。D是离题选项：转去评论第三人"她"很累，没有回应对方的关心。

items.situation: friend sharing news or personal update
items.speaker: Why didn't you tell me you got the job?

options.A: The job is downtown near the station.
options.B: No, I haven't told her anything.
options.C: Tell me where you parked the car.
options.D: Honestly, I wanted to surprise you.

items.explanation: 说话人责怪式地问"为什么没告诉我你拿到工作了"，是在分享好消息后的互动。正确答案D以话语标记"Honestly"开头，间接说明原因——想给对方惊喜，属于marker_led_indirect。A是语义关联陷阱：抓住"job"去说工作地点，没回答"为什么"。B是错误问题类型：把问题当成是非问回答"没有"，且对象错成"她"。C是语义关联陷阱兼一词多义：复用"tell"但变成命令对方说停车位置。

items.situation: student checking lost-and-found at front desk
items.speaker: I think I left my backpack here yesterday.

options.A: What color is it?
options.B: The desk closes at five.
options.C: I packed my bag this morning.
options.D: Yes, yesterday was pretty busy.

items.explanation: 学生在失物招领处说昨天落了背包。正确答案A以反问推进对话——询问颜色以便查找，属于counter_question。B是离题选项：说前台关门时间，没帮忙找包。C是语义关联陷阱：抓住"backpack/bag"去说自己今早收拾包，与失物无关。D是离题选项：附和"昨天很忙"，对找包毫无帮助。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's situation
items.speaker: What are you going to do about your rent?

options.A: The apartment is on the fifth floor.
options.B: I can lend you some money if you need.
options.C: Rent has gone up everywhere lately.
options.D: When did you sign the lease?

items.explanation: 说话人关切地问朋友"房租怎么办"，潜台词是担心对方付不起。正确答案B不直接答"怎么办"，而是主动提出借钱解决根本问题，属于context_shift。A是语义关联陷阱：抓住"rent"联想到公寓楼层，答非所问。C是语义关联陷阱兼离题：泛泛评论"房租到处都涨"，没回应对方的处境。D是错误问题类型：用反问"何时签约"转移话题，没解决当前困境。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: Where should I wait for you tomorrow?

options.A: I waited for almost an hour.
options.B: Tomorrow is going to be sunny.
options.C: Right by the main entrance works.
options.D: Yes, I'll be driving the blue car.

items.explanation: 说话人安排拼车，问明天在哪等。正确答案C用口语化的"works"（这个地点可行）自然给出地点——主入口旁，属于idiomatic且直接推进。A是语义关联陷阱兼一词多义：复用"wait"但说的是过去等了一小时。B是离题选项：抓住"tomorrow"去说天气，没回答地点。D是错误问题类型：把"在哪"答成是非问，给出车的信息而非地点。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: Are you free to meet sometime this week?

options.A: The meeting room is down the hall.
options.B: Yes, the assignment was due last week.
options.C: I felt free all summer long.
options.D: Well, my Thursday afternoon is open.

items.explanation: 学生问教授本周是否有空见面。正确答案D以话语标记"Well"开头，间接给出可行时段——周四下午有空，属于marker_led_indirect。A是语义关联陷阱：抓住"meet"联想到会议室位置，没回答是否有空。B是错误问题类型兼一词多义：复用"week"但答成作业截止时间，文不对题。C是语义关联陷阱兼一词多义：把"free"（有空）误用为"自由/无事"形容整个夏天。

items.situation: professor responding to a student question after lecture
items.speaker: Why is my thesis statement still too weak?

options.A: Have you tried making a clearer claim?
options.B: The thesis is due at the end of term.
options.C: I write all my statements by hand.
options.D: Yes, weak coffee keeps me up at night.

items.explanation: 学生课后问教授论文论点为何仍然不够有力。正确答案A以反问推进对话——引导学生提出更清晰的主张，属于counter_question。B是语义关联陷阱：抓住"thesis"去说截止日期，没解释为何薄弱。C是语义关联陷阱兼一词多义：复用"statement"但理解成"手写陈述"。D是语义关联陷阱兼一词多义：把"weak"（薄弱）误用为形容"淡咖啡"，完全离题。

items.situation: academic advisor reassuring a student about course load
items.speaker: I don't think I can handle five classes.

options.A: Let's drop one and lighten your schedule.
options.B: The classroom is on the second floor.
options.C: I handled the package very carefully.
options.D: Yes, five is a nice round number.

items.explanation: 学生向顾问坦言觉得五门课吃不消，潜台词是想减负。正确答案A不停留在表态，而是直接解决问题——退掉一门减轻负担，属于context_shift。B是语义关联陷阱：抓住"classes"联想到教室位置，答非所问。C是语义关联陷阱兼一词多义：复用"handle"但理解成"搬运包裹"。D是离题选项：附和"五是个好数字"，对学生的焦虑毫无帮助。

items.situation: librarian responding to a student returning a book
items.speaker: Is there a fine for returning this late?

options.A: The book was a really fine read.
options.B: Yes, the library closes at nine.
options.C: The shelves are fine just as they are.
options.D: Actually, we waived late fees this term.

items.explanation: 学生还书时问逾期是否有罚款。正确答案D以话语标记"Actually"开头，间接给出好消息——本学期免除逾期费，属于marker_led_indirect。A是一词多义陷阱：把"fine"（罚款）误用为"很好的"形容读物。B是错误问题类型：答成图书馆关门时间，没回答是否罚款。C是一词多义陷阱：再次把"fine"误用为"挺好"形容书架。

items.situation: coach checking in with a tired team member
items.speaker: You seem pretty worn out after practice.

options.A: Maybe sit out the next drill and rest.
options.B: My shoes are completely worn out.
options.C: Practice starts at six tomorrow.
options.D: Yes, the field was muddy today.

items.explanation: 教练观察到队员练完很疲惫，潜台词是关心其状态。正确答案A不停留在表态，而是直接解决问题——建议休息一轮，属于context_shift。B是语义关联陷阱兼一词多义：复用"worn out"但理解成"鞋子磨破了"。C是语义关联陷阱：抓住"practice"去说明天训练时间，没回应疲惫。D是离题选项：附和"场地很泥泞"，没回应队员的状态。

items.situation: resident assistant addressing a noise complaint with a student
items.speaker: Could you keep the music down after ten?

options.A: Would headphones be okay instead?
options.B: The music festival is next month.
options.C: I keep my room very tidy.
options.D: Yes, ten dollars sounds fair.

items.explanation: 宿管请学生十点后把音乐音量调小。正确答案A以反问推进协商——提议改用耳机，既配合又给出方案，属于counter_question。B是语义关联陷阱：抓住"music"去说音乐节，答非所问。C是语义关联陷阱兼一词多义：复用"keep"但变成"保持房间整洁"。D是错误问题类型兼一词多义：把"ten"（十点）误当成"十美元"来回答，文不对题。

items.situation: classmate asking about a missed study group
items.speaker: What did you guys cover in study group?

options.A: I can send you my notes tonight.
options.B: The cover of the book is torn.
options.C: Our group has about six people.
options.D: When does the group meet next?

items.explanation: 同学问"学习小组讲了什么"，潜台词是想补上错过的内容。正确答案A不逐条复述，而是直接解决需求——把笔记发过去，属于context_shift。B是一词多义陷阱：把"cover"（讲解）误用为"书的封面"。C是语义关联陷阱：抓住"group"去说人数，没回答讲了什么。D是错误问题类型：用反问"下次何时聚"转移话题，没回应当前问题。

items.situation: front desk staff helping a student with a room key
items.speaker: My key card stopped working this morning.

options.A: The morning shift starts at eight.
options.B: I worked really hard this morning.
options.C: Let me print you a new one right now.
options.D: She lost her keys last week too.

items.explanation: 学生在前台反映门卡今早失灵。正确答案C直接回应并解决——立刻补办一张新卡，自然推进对话。A是语义关联陷阱：抓住"morning"去说早班时间，没解决门卡问题。B是语义关联陷阱兼一词多义：复用"work/morning"但说成"今早工作很努力"。D是离题选项：转去说第三人"她"上周也丢钥匙，没帮当前学生。

items.situation: advisor discussing an internship application with a student
items.speaker: Did you submit the internship application yet?

options.A: The intern sits at that front desk.
options.B: Yes, applications make great wall art.
options.C: I submitted to the urge for a nap.
options.D: Not quite, I'm still polishing my resume.

items.explanation: 顾问问学生实习申请是否已提交。正确答案D以"Not quite"婉转作答，间接说明进度——还在完善简历，属于marker_led_indirect。A是语义关联陷阱：抓住"internship"去说实习生坐在哪，没回答是否提交。B是离题选项：把"applications"扯成"墙面装饰"，荒诞离题。C是一词多义陷阱：把"submit"（提交）误用为"屈服于（睡意）"，文不对题。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: What format do you want the lab report in?

options.A: Just follow the template on the course page.
options.B: The lab was canceled last Tuesday.
options.C: I formatted my whole laptop yesterday.
options.D: Reports are due at the end of term.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用context_shift策略——不纠结于具体格式细节，而是直接指向课程页面的模板解决学生的根本需求，自然且有帮助。B是off_topic干扰项，谈论实验被取消，与格式问题无关。C是polysemy陷阱，把'format'当作'格式化电脑硬盘'的意思，是一词多义误用。D是wrong_question_type，学生问的是'什么格式'（how/format），D却回答了'什么时候交'（when），答非所问。

items.situation: asking for directions to a nearby location
items.speaker: Do you know how to get to the post office?

options.A: I mailed that package already.
options.B: Are you going by car or walking?
options.C: The office closes at five today.
options.D: Yes, I sent a letter there once.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用counter_question策略，反问对方是开车还是步行，从而能给出更准确的路线，推动对话前进。A是semantic_association_trap，'post office'联想到'mail/package'，谈论自己寄过包裹，不回答路线。C是off_topic，回答了营业时间（when），而非如何到达（how）。D是审核中标记的危险模式——'Yes, I sent a letter there once'看似自然，但它暗示说话者知道地点却不给路线，属于off_topic回避，且'letter/there'是语义关联陷阱。

items.situation: student seeking help at the writing center
items.speaker: I'm not sure my introduction is strong enough.

options.A: The library closes at midnight tonight.
options.B: Introductions are the easiest part.
options.C: Well, let's read through it together.
options.D: I introduced myself to the new tutor.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用marker_led_indirect策略，以话语标记'Well'开头，提议一起读一遍，间接而有帮助地回应了学生的担忧。A是off_topic，谈论图书馆关门时间，与开头段毫无关系。B是misinterpretation，反驳说开头最简单，没有回应学生想要帮助的实际需求，且语气不当。D是semantic_association_trap，把'introduction'（文章开头）误作'introduce oneself'（自我介绍），一词多义误用。

items.situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: Did you ever get the heater fixed?

options.A: The dorm has a new lounge downstairs.
options.B: I prefer the heat turned up high.
options.C: Yes, you can fix it yourself easily.
options.D: Finally, yes — it's working now.

items.explanation: 正确答案D用idiomatic策略，以'Finally'这一自然口语开头确认暖气终于修好了，符合对话语境。A是off_topic，谈论宿舍新休息室，与暖气是否修好无关。B是semantic_association_trap，'heater'联想到'heat'，谈个人偏好而不回答是否修好。C是wrong_question_type，问的是'你修好了吗'（是否完成），C却回答'你可以自己修'（指导如何修），答非所问。

items.situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed
items.speaker: Why are you taking all this on alone?

options.A: Honestly, I didn't want to bother anyone.
options.B: The train was alone on the track.
options.C: Take the second one on the left.
options.D: I took three classes last semester.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用idiomatic策略，以'Honestly'这一自然口语标记开头，坦白解释自己不想麻烦别人，真实回应了'为什么独自承担'。B是semantic_association_trap，重复了'alone'一词却用在完全无关的火车语境。C是wrong_question_type，回答了'往哪走'（where/方向），而非'为什么'（why）。D是semantic_association_trap，重复了'take/took'一词，谈上学期选课，与当前情境无关。

items.situation: student asking about gym or sports facility hours
items.speaker: Where do I sign up for the gym?

options.A: The gym has brand-new treadmills.
options.B: Actually, you just do it online now.
options.C: Sign-ups closed two weeks ago.
options.D: I work out almost every morning.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用marker_led_indirect策略，以'Actually'开头，间接告知现在改为线上注册，有效回应了'在哪注册'的需求。A是semantic_association_trap，'gym'联想到'treadmills'，描述健身房设备而不回答在哪注册。C是off_topic，回答注册已截止（when），而非在哪注册（where）。D是off_topic，'I work out every morning'是审核中标记的危险模式——看似自然却是分享个人习惯，回避了问题。

items.situation: student asking about printing or scanning at computer lab
items.speaker: How do I scan these pages to my email?

options.A: The lab installed faster computers.
options.B: Scanners use a lot of toner.
options.C: Here, let me set it up for you.
options.D: Email me the file when you're done.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用context_shift策略——不逐步解释如何扫描，而是直接提出帮对方设置好，解决了根本需求，自然且热心。A是off_topic，谈论实验室换了更快的电脑，与扫描操作无关。B是semantic_association_trap，'scan'联想到'scanner/toner'，陈述关于扫描仪的事实却不帮忙。D是misinterpretation，把情境反转，让对方扫完发邮件给自己，误解了说话者是求助方。

items.situation: making or rescheduling an appointment
items.speaker: I'd like to move my appointment to Friday.

options.A: The dentist has a lovely waiting room.
options.B: I moved into my new place last week.
options.C: Appointments usually take an hour.
options.D: Sure, Friday at two works fine.

items.explanation: 正确答案D用direct_topical策略，直接确认周五两点可以，正面回应了改约请求。A是off_topic，谈论牙医候诊室，与改约无关。B是semantic_association_trap，重复了'move/moved'一词，却用在'搬家'的语义上，一词多义误用。C是off_topic，陈述预约通常的时长，与改到周五的请求无关。

items.situation: professor reminding student about a missed deadline
items.speaker: Your draft was supposed to be in yesterday.

options.A: Actually, I emailed it last night.
options.B: Drafts are usually double-spaced.
options.C: Yesterday was a beautiful day outside.
options.D: I draft all my essays by hand.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用marker_led_indirect策略，以'Actually'开头，间接澄清自己昨晚已发邮件提交，恰当回应了教授的提醒。B是semantic_association_trap，'draft'联想到格式规范，陈述草稿通常双倍行距，回避了迟交问题。C是semantic_association_trap，重复了'yesterday'却谈天气，完全无关。D是semantic_association_trap，重复了'draft'一词谈论自己手写习惯，与是否按时交无关。

items.situation: student asking advisor which course to take next term
items.speaker: Which elective should I register for next?

options.A: I registered my car on campus already.
options.B: What are you hoping to focus on later?
options.C: Electives are worth three credits each.
options.D: The registrar's office is closed today.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用counter_question策略，反问学生未来想专注的方向，从而能给出更贴合的选课建议，推动对话前进。A是semantic_association_trap，重复了'register/registered'一词，却用在'给车登记'的语义上。C是off_topic，陈述选修课的学分，与该选哪门无关。D是off_topic，谈注册办公室今天关门，回避了具体推荐。

items.situation: classmate asking about borrowing lecture notes
items.speaker: Can I borrow your notes from Monday's class?

options.A: Of course, I'll send them right over.
options.B: I take notes on my tablet now.
options.C: The note on the door said it's closed.
options.D: Did you write that down somewhere?

items.explanation: 正确答案A用idiomatic策略，以'Of course'这一自然口语爽快答应，并说马上发过去，恰当回应了借笔记的请求。B是semantic_association_trap，'notes'联想到记笔记方式，谈自己用平板，回避了是否能借。C是semantic_association_trap，'note'被误用为'门上的告示'，一词多义陷阱。D是wrong_question_type，问'你写下来没有'，把发问者当成被问的人，误解了情境。

items.situation: student asking librarian about extending a book loan
items.speaker: Can I keep this book another two weeks?

options.A: Let me just renew it for you now.
options.B: That book has a torn cover.
options.C: We added two new study rooms.
options.D: The author wrote three other books.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用context_shift策略——不简单回答'可以/不可以'，而是直接帮对方续借，解决了根本需求，自然高效。B是semantic_association_trap，'book'联想到书的破损，描述书况却不处理续借。C是off_topic，谈新增自习室，与续借无关。D是semantic_association_trap，'book'联想到作者其他著作，陈述无关事实。

items.situation: coach checking on an athlete who missed practice
items.speaker: How come you skipped practice this morning?

options.A: The field was repainted over the break.
options.B: Practice makes you a better player.
options.C: Well, I had a doctor's appointment.
options.D: I practice my serve every weekend.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用marker_led_indirect策略，以'Well'开头，间接解释因为有医生预约而缺席，恰当回应了教练的询问。A是off_topic，谈球场重新刷漆，与缺席原因无关。B是semantic_association_trap，'practice'联想到'练习使人进步'的格言，答非所问。D是semantic_association_trap，重复了'practice'一词谈周末练发球，与今早为何缺席无关。

items.situation: RA asking student about a noise complaint
items.speaker: What was all that noise last night?

options.A: Sorry, I'll keep it down from now on.
options.B: The walls here are pretty thin.
options.C: Night classes are hard to stay awake in.
options.D: I made some noise complaints last year.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用context_shift策略——不解释噪音是什么，而是直接道歉并承诺以后保持安静，解决了RA关心的根本问题。B是semantic_association_trap，'noise'联想到隔音差，把责任推给墙薄而不正面回应。C是semantic_association_trap，重复了'night'却谈夜间课程犯困，完全无关。D是semantic_association_trap，重复了'noise'一词谈自己去年投诉过，把情境反转，误解了发问方。

items.situation: staff member telling student about a parking permit
items.speaker: Your parking permit expires at the end of May.

options.A: The parking lot was repaved recently.
options.B: I parked closer to the gym today.
options.C: Permits cost more for visitors.
options.D: Thanks — I'll renew it this week.

items.explanation: 正确答案D用direct_topical策略，直接致谢并表示本周会续办，正面回应了停车证到期的提醒。A是semantic_association_trap，'parking'联想到停车场重铺，陈述无关事实。B是semantic_association_trap，重复了'park/parked'一词谈今天停车位置，与到期提醒无关。C是off_topic，谈访客停车证更贵，回避了续办自己的证件。

items.situation: librarian offering to help a student find a resource
items.speaker: Are you finding everything you need today?

options.A: Actually, could you help me with this?
options.B: I found my keys under the desk.
options.C: The needle was hard to find.
options.D: Yes, I need to study for finals.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用idiomatic/marker_led策略，以'Actually'开头自然地把馆员的客套询问转为求助，真实推进对话。B是semantic_association_trap，重复了'find/found'一词却谈找到钥匙，语义无关。C是semantic_association_trap，'finding'联想到'needle'（大海捞针），用在无关语境。D是misinterpretation——'Yes, I need to study for finals'看似回应，但'need'被误接成自己的备考需求，没有回答是否找到所需资料，属审核中标记的危险模式（顺着关键词给看似相关却答非所问的回答）。

items.situation: classmate asks professor how long the office hours run
items.speaker: How long are your office hours today?

options.A: What time works best for you?
options.B: My office is on the second floor.
options.C: Yes, I hold them every week.
options.D: The class ran a bit long today.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用反问推进对话——教授不直接报时长，而是反问学生哪个时间方便，把安排落到实处，符合counter_question范式。B是语义联想陷阱：抓住office只描述办公室位置，没回答时长。C是答非所问：回答了yes/no而非How long问的时长。D是离题但合语法：抓住today说课拖堂，与办公时间无关。

items.situation: student asks advisor how to drop a course
items.speaker: How do I drop this course before the deadline?

options.A: Have you talked to the professor yet?
options.B: The course covers a lot of material.
options.C: Yes, the deadline is coming up fast.
options.D: I dropped my umbrella somewhere.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用反问推进——顾问先确认学生是否已和教授沟通，这是退课流程的关键一步，属counter_question范式。B是语义联想陷阱：抓住course只评论课程内容多，没说怎么退。C是答非所问：回答yes/no并重复deadline，未解释操作步骤。D是多义/误解陷阱：把drop误读成'掉落'，说丢了伞，完全跑题。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend who looks exhausted
items.speaker: Have you been getting any sleep lately?

options.A: Sleep is important for memory.
options.B: Let me cover your shift tonight.
options.C: Yes, I bought a new pillow online.
options.D: The store closes at nine tonight.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用context_shift范式——不直接回答睡没睡，而是直接解决朋友疲惫的根源，主动提出帮忙顶班，让对方能休息。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住sleep泛谈睡眠对记忆的重要性，像背书，不回应关心。C是语义联想陷阱：抓住sleep扯到买枕头，且只答yes加无关细节。D是离题但合语法：说商店九点关门，与睡眠和关心毫无关联。

items.situation: friend worried about a classmate who failed an exam
items.speaker: Are you doing okay after that exam result?

options.A: Exams are graded on a curve.
options.B: Let's study together this weekend.
options.C: Yes, the exam had four sections.
options.D: I left my calculator at home.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用context_shift范式——不停留在'我还好'的表面回答，而是直击成绩不理想的根源，提议周末一起复习来扭转局面。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住exam谈曲线评分规则，回避了对方的关心。C是答非所问加语义陷阱：只回yes并复述考试结构，没回应情绪。D是离题但合语法：说把计算器忘家里，是另一场对话里的话。

items.situation: student asks staff if the recreation center is open late
items.speaker: Is the rec center open this late on weekdays?

options.A: The pool was just renovated last month.
options.B: No, I prefer working out in the morning.
options.C: Actually, it closes at eleven tonight.
options.D: Yes, the treadmills are very popular.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用marker_led_indirect范式——以话语标记Actually开头，间接给出有用信息：今晚十一点关门，等于回应了'是否还开着'。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住rec center谈泳池翻新，没说开放时间。B是答非所问加个人偏好：用No但讲自己爱早上锻炼，没回答场馆是否开。D是语义联想陷阱：抓住健身器材的受欢迎度，回避了时间问题。

items.situation: student asks coach whether the gym is open during the holiday
items.speaker: Are we allowed in the gym over the break?

options.A: The new lockers were installed yesterday.
options.B: No, I usually train at a different gym.
options.C: Well, you'll need to swipe your ID card.
options.D: Yes, the team won three games last season.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用marker_led_indirect范式——以Well开头，间接确认假期可进但需刷ID卡，既回答了'是否允许'又给出条件。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住gym谈新储物柜安装，没回答能否进。B是答非所问：用No讲自己常去别的健身房，与校内场馆是否开放无关。D是离题但合语法：抓住team扯到球队战绩，与假期开放无关。

items.situation: friend asks why the other is driving everyone instead of carpooling
items.speaker: Why don't we just split the driving tomorrow?

options.A: The car needs an oil change soon.
options.B: Driving at night makes me nervous.
options.C: No, the highway was closed yesterday.
options.D: Sounds good to me.

items.explanation: 正确答案D用idiomatic范式——'Sounds good to me'是自然的口语套语，表示欣然同意轮流开车，顺势推进安排。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住car谈该换机油，没回应分担提议。B是离题但合语法：说自己怕夜里开车，像另一段对话，未回应明天的分工。C是答非所问加误解：用No并扯昨天高速封路，与'要不要分担'无关。

items.situation: friend asks why the other won't share a ride to the station
items.speaker: Why not ride together to the station?

options.A: The station was crowded this morning.
options.B: Trains are usually on time here.
options.C: No, I haven't bought my ticket.
options.D: Works for me.

items.explanation: 正确答案D用idiomatic范式——'Works for me'是地道的口语应答，表示同意一起搭车前往车站，顺势答应。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住station说早上很挤，没回应一起走的提议。B是语义联想陷阱：抓住station扯到火车准点，与拼车无关。C是答非所问：用No讲还没买票，回避了'要不要一起'这个问题。

items.situation: student tells the RA about a broken radiator in the dorm
items.speaker: The radiator in my room won't turn off.

options.A: I'll get someone on it right away.
options.B: My room faces the courtyard.
options.C: The heating bill went up this year.
options.D: Did you try the dining hall?

items.explanation: 正确答案A用idiomatic范式——'I'll get someone on it right away'是地道的口语承诺，表示马上派人来修暖气，正面回应了报修。B是语义联想陷阱：抓住room只说朝向庭院，与暖气故障无关。C是语义联想陷阱：抓住radiator联想到取暖费上涨，回避了维修需求。D是离题但合语法：问去没去食堂，与报修暖气完全不搭。

items.situation: student tells staff the dorm laundry machine ate their money
items.speaker: The washer took my coins but never started.

options.A: Let me sort that out for you.
options.B: The laundry room is down the hall.
options.C: Quarters work best in those machines.
options.D: I washed my jacket this morning.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用idiomatic范式——'Let me sort that out for you'是地道口语，表示我来帮你处理这个问题，直接回应了投币却没启动的故障。B是语义联想陷阱：抓住washer/laundry只说洗衣房在走廊尽头，没解决问题。C是语义联想陷阱：抓住coins扯到用25美分硬币最好用，回避了报修。D是离题但合语法：说自己早上洗了夹克，是无关的个人小事。

items.situation: student tells the librarian the reading list isn't on reserve
items.speaker: I can't find the reading list for this course.

options.A: The library opens at eight tomorrow.
options.B: Well, it should be posted on the course page.
options.C: Reading every night is a good habit.
options.D: That author has written many books.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用marker_led_indirect范式——以Well开头，间接指路：阅读清单应该挂在课程页面上，给了找到它的实际办法。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住library/find只报开馆时间，没说清单在哪。C是语义联想陷阱：抓住reading泛谈每晚阅读是好习惯，跑偏。D是离题但合语法：抓住reading list联想到作者写了很多书，与找清单无关。

items.situation: student tells the professor the syllabus reading list seems incomplete
items.speaker: The reading list only shows three books.

options.A: Books can be expensive these days.
options.B: Actually, the rest go up next week.
options.C: The bookstore is across the street.
options.D: I enjoy reading historical novels.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用marker_led_indirect范式——以Actually开头，间接说明：其余书目下周才会发布，解释了清单为何只有三本。A是离题但合语法：泛谈书很贵，与清单是否完整无关。C是语义联想陷阱：抓住books联想到书店位置，没回应数量问题。D是离题但合语法：说自己爱读历史小说，是无关的个人偏好。

items.situation: two friends deciding where to grab lunch on campus
items.speaker: What sounds good for lunch today?

options.A: Lunch is the most important meal.
options.B: I ate there last Tuesday.
options.C: Maybe the new place by the library.
options.D: Yes, I'm pretty hungry now.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用marker_led_indirect范式——以Maybe开头，间接给出建议：图书馆旁新开的那家，正面推进'去哪吃'的讨论。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住lunch泛谈午餐是最重要的一餐，没给地点。B是离题但合语法：说自己上周二在那吃过，没回应今天去哪。D是答非所问：用Yes回应饿不饿，可问的是吃什么/去哪，答错了问题类型。

items.situation: two classmates choosing a spot for a quick lunch
items.speaker: What do you feel like eating today?

options.A: Eating slowly helps digestion.
options.B: I forgot my wallet upstairs.
options.C: Maybe something quick from the café.
options.D: Yes, let's eat soon.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用marker_led_indirect范式——以Maybe开头，间接提议：从咖啡馆买点快的，正面回应'想吃什么'。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住eating泛谈细嚼慢咽助消化，跑题。B是离题但合语法：说把钱包忘楼上了，像另一段对话的内容。D是答非所问：用Yes回应'要不要快点吃'，可问的是吃什么，答错了问题类型。

items.situation: coordinating an airport pickup spot with a friend
items.speaker: Where should I wait for you at the airport?

options.A: The airport added a new food court.
options.B: My flight has a window seat.
options.C: Departures gets crowded around noon.
options.D: Don't worry, I'll just take a cab home.

items.explanation: 正确答案D用context_shift范式——不纠结在哪等，而是直接化解接机这件事本身：别担心，我自己打车回家，从根本上解决了对方的麻烦。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住airport说新增了美食广场，与等候地点无关。B是语义联想陷阱：抓住flight谈靠窗座位，跑偏到航班细节。C是离题但合语法：说出发层中午很挤，看似相关却没回答该在哪等，属无用信息。

items.situation: arranging where to meet a friend at the airport terminal
items.speaker: Where exactly are you landing this evening?

options.A: The airport parking is pricey lately.
options.B: Evening flights are often delayed.
options.C: I packed a heavy suitcase this time.
options.D: Don't bother coming, I'll meet you home.

items.explanation: 正确答案D用context_shift范式——不报降落地点，而是直接解决接机这件事的核心：不用来了，我们家里见，免去对方跑机场。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住airport说停车费近来很贵，与降落地点无关。B是语义联想陷阱：抓住evening联想到晚间航班常延误，回避了'在哪降落'。C是离题但合语法：说这次行李很重，是无关的个人细节。

items.situation: student asking professor whether a draft is due before the holiday
items.speaker: Is the rough draft due before the break?

options.A: The break is two weeks long this year.
options.B: I broke my laptop screen last week.
options.C: Actually, you've got until the Monday after.
options.D: Yes, I've already started writing mine.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用话语标记词Actually开头，间接地告知截止时间在假期后的周一，直接回应了学生的疑问。A是语义联想陷阱（抓住break谈论假期长度，却没回答截止时间）；B是谐音/多义陷阱（break被误用为弄坏broke，且与作业无关）；D是自然回应陷阱式错误——它分享了说话者的处境（我也开始写了），这种共情式回答看似合理却没有提供截止信息，属于应避免的干扰项类型。

items.situation: advisor asking student about course registration progress
items.speaker: Have you signed up for next semester's classes yet?

options.A: Don't worry, I'll walk you through it now.
options.B: The semester starts in early September.
options.C: Yes, the classroom is on the second floor.
options.D: No, I haven't seen the new schedule.

items.explanation: 正确答案A采用情境转移范式——顾问没有停留在是否注册的字面问题上，而是直接解决学生背后的需求（我现在带你一步步弄）。B是语义联想陷阱（抓住semester谈论开学时间）；C是语义联想陷阱（抓住classes/class联想到教室位置，答非所问）；D虽以No开头看似合理，但属于错误情境——它把问句当成你看没看新课表，回应了错误的问题。

items.situation: classmate asking where to meet before a study group
items.speaker: Where are we meeting before the study group?

options.A: The group has about six people now.
options.B: We studied really hard for that one.
options.C: Let's meet by the cafe entrance.
options.D: I joined the group last semester.

items.explanation: 正确答案C直接回答了地点问题（在咖啡馆入口碰头），符合direct_topical范式。A是语义联想陷阱（抓住group谈论小组人数，答非所问）；B是语义联想陷阱（抓住study/studied谈论备考，且时态错位）；D是离题但合乎语法的句子（谈论何时加入小组，与碰头地点无关）。

items.situation: staff member telling a student the printing quota is used up
items.speaker: You've used up your printing quota for the month.

options.A: Is there a way to add more credits?
options.B: The printer is down the hall.
options.C: I printed those slides yesterday.
options.D: Yes, the month went by so fast.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用反问推进对话（有没有办法加额度），积极应对了配额用尽的情况，符合counter_question范式。B是语义联想陷阱（抓住printing联想到打印机位置，答非所问）；C是语义联想陷阱（抓住printing/printed谈论昨天打印的事，与当前配额无关）；D是离题回应（附和月份过得快，没有处理配额问题）。

items.situation: librarian asking if a student needs help locating a journal
items.speaker: Can I help you track down that article?

options.A: The article was published last spring.
options.B: Yes, I track my reading every week.
options.C: Actually, I just found it myself.
options.D: No, the printer ran out of paper.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用话语标记词Actually开头，间接表明不需要帮助（我自己刚找到了），自然推进对话，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A是语义联想陷阱（抓住article谈论发表时间，答非所问）；B是多义陷阱（track被误用为追踪阅读进度，而非原句的找到article）；D虽以No开头看似拒绝，但属于错误情境——把帮助找文章误解为某个打印机缺纸的问题，离题。

items.situation: coach asking why a player missed warm-ups
items.speaker: Why weren't you at warm-ups this morning?

options.A: Warm weather always makes me tired.
options.B: The morning session runs until ten.
options.C: Yes, I usually warm up before games.
options.D: My alarm didn't go off, I'm really sorry.

items.explanation: 正确答案D采用情境转移范式——没有停留在为什么的解释上空谈，而是给出真实原因并道歉解决了教练关心的问题（闹钟没响，很抱歉）。A是语义联想陷阱（把warm-ups的warm误联想到warm weather暖天气）；B是语义联想陷阱（抓住morning谈论训练时长，答非所问）；C是错误问题类型（用Yes回答一个why问题，且谈论赛前热身习惯，答非所问）。

items.situation: professor asking what topic a student picked for the paper
items.speaker: What angle are you taking for your paper?

options.A: I'm still on the fence about it.
options.B: The paper is due in two weeks.
options.C: I bought new paper for the printer.
options.D: Yes, I'm taking three classes this term.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用习语on the fence（还在犹豫不决）自然回应了选题问题，符合idiomatic范式。B是语义联想陷阱（抓住paper谈论截止日期，答非所问）；C是多义陷阱（paper被误用为打印纸张，而非论文）；D是错误问题类型（用Yes回答一个what问题，且taking被误用为选课）。

items.situation: friend arranging a shared ride to the conference
items.speaker: Why don't we just take one car downtown?

options.A: Downtown gets pretty crowded on weekends.
options.B: The car wash is closed on Mondays.
options.C: Yes, I drive downtown all the time.
options.D: Sounds good, I'll swing by your place at eight.

items.explanation: 正确答案D采用情境转移范式——没有讨论为什么的字面问题，而是直接接受提议并安排接送细节（好啊，我八点去你家接你），推进了实际计划。A是语义联想陷阱（抓住downtown谈论市中心拥挤，没回应拼车提议）；B是语义联想陷阱（抓住car联想到洗车店，答非所问）；C是错误问题类型（用Yes回答一个why don't we提议，且谈论自己常开车，没接受共乘）。

items.situation: RA reminding a resident about quiet hours
items.speaker: Quiet hours start at eleven on weeknights.

options.A: Got it, I'll keep the music down.
options.B: The hours feel really long this week.
options.C: I stayed up till eleven studying.
options.D: Yes, the weekend went by quietly.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用Got it开头间接表示理解并承诺配合（我会把音乐调小），自然回应了RA的提醒，符合marker_led_indirect范式。B是语义联想陷阱（抓住hours谈论时间漫长，答非所问）；C是语义联想陷阱（抓住eleven谈论自己学到几点，与遵守安静时段无关）；D是离题回应（附和周末很安静，但提醒说的是工作日，时间错位且没处理要求）。

items.situation: classmate asking if a lab partner finished the report section
items.speaker: Did you finish your part of the lab report?

options.A: The lab is on the fourth floor.
options.B: Reports are due every Friday.
options.C: Yes, the report has four sections.
options.D: Just about, give me ten more minutes.

items.explanation: 正确答案D用话语标记词Just开头表示即将完成（差不多了，再给我十分钟），间接而具体地回应了是否完成，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A是语义联想陷阱（抓住lab联想到实验室楼层，答非所问）；B是语义联想陷阱（抓住report谈论交作业的周期，没回应是否完成）；C是错误问题类型——用Yes回答后却谈论报告有几部分，答非所问，且属于看似确认实则不切题的回应。

items.situation: professor offering feedback on a thesis statement
items.speaker: Your thesis needs a sharper focus.

options.A: Which part feels too broad to you?
options.B: The focus ring on my camera broke.
options.C: Yes, I typed the thesis last night.
options.D: Theses are usually pretty long.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用反问推进对话（您觉得哪部分太宽泛），积极寻求具体反馈，符合counter_question范式。B是多义陷阱（focus被误用为相机的对焦环，而非论点焦点）；C是错误问题类型（用Yes回应一个建议，且谈论昨晚打字，答非所问）；D是语义联想陷阱（抓住thesis谈论论文长度，与需要更聚焦无关）。

items.situation: coach asking a player how a sore knee feels before practice
items.speaker: How's that knee holding up today?

options.A: Much better, thanks for checking.
options.B: The practice today runs pretty late.
options.C: I held the door for the team.
options.D: Yes, the gym floor is brand new.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用自然的口语回应（好多了，谢谢关心）直接而温暖地回答了膝盖状况，符合idiomatic的会话套语风格。B是语义联想陷阱（抓住today谈论训练时长，没回应膝盖）；C是多义陷阱（hold/held被从holding up状况误用为held the door扶门）；D是错误问题类型（用Yes回答一个how问题，且谈论体育馆地板，答非所问）。

items.situation: librarian telling a student a requested book is now available
items.speaker: The book you reserved just came in.

options.A: Great, I'll swing by to grab it.
options.B: The library closes early on Fridays.
options.C: I read that book a while ago.
options.D: Yes, the shelves are pretty full.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用Great开头间接表示乐意取书（太好了，我顺路过来拿），自然推进对话，符合marker_led_indirect范式。B是语义联想陷阱（抓住book/library联想到图书馆闭馆时间，答非所问）；C是离题但合乎语法的句子（谈论自己早就读过那本书，与取预约书无关，甚至自相矛盾）；D是错误问题类型（用Yes回应一个陈述句通知，且谈论书架很满，答非所问）。

items.situation: friend coordinating who drives to the airport
items.speaker: My flight gets in around midnight.

options.A: The airport food court is expensive.
options.B: No problem, I'll be there to grab you.
options.C: Flights are often cheaper at night.
options.D: I flew in late last weekend too.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用会话套语No problem开头主动解决接机需求（没问题，我去接你），符合idiomatic范式，且回应了航班深夜到达的潜在请求。A是语义联想陷阱（抓住flight/airport联想到机场餐饮贵，答非所问）；C是语义联想陷阱（抓住flight谈论夜间机票便宜，没回应接机）；D是离题回应，且属于应避免的共情式干扰——它分享了自己也曾深夜到达的经历，看似自然却没有解决接机问题。

items.situation: friend inviting another to a study group or social event
items.speaker: How about we make a night of it after the chemistry midterm?

options.A: Count me in — I could use the break after that exam.
options.B: The chemistry textbook is on the second shelf.
options.C: I made an excellent night soup last weekend.
options.D: Yes, the midterm covers chapters four through nine.

items.explanation: 说话人在用习语式邀请"make a night of it"，A 用 "Count me in" 习语表达欣然接受，并连接上下文（考试后想放松）。B 抓住 chemistry 做语义陷阱，semantic_association_trap。C 把 "make a night" 误读成做菜，polysemy/misinterpretation。D 把发起邀请误当作问中期内容，wrong_question_type。

items.situation: student discussing a presentation topic with partner
items.speaker: What do you think about narrowing our presentation to just the case study from chapter three?

options.A: Yes, the auditorium has a great projector.
options.B: Honestly, I think focusing on one case will make our argument much sharper.
options.C: Chapter three is right after the introduction.
options.D: I didn't finish reading the textbook this week.

items.explanation: 说话人征求意见，B 用 "Honestly" 标记表态并解释为何赞同，符合 marker_led_indirect 模式。A 答会议室设备，off_topic。C 描述章节位置，semantic_association_trap。D 转移到自己没读书的话题，off_topic 但是回避问题。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: The drumming next door has been driving me up the wall this week.

options.A: The wall is freshly painted.
options.B: I bought a new drum last month.
options.C: Same here — I might leave them a note this evening.
options.D: Yes, the wall is twelve feet tall.

items.explanation: 说话人用习语 "driving me up the wall" 抱怨噪音。C 表示同感并提出实际行动，呼应抱怨的语用意图。A 抓住 "wall" 字面意思，polysemy/literal trap。B 用 drum 做语义联想陷阱。D 答墙的高度，wrong_question_type。

items.situation: asking a store clerk about a return or exchange
items.speaker: Where can I go to get the ball rolling on a return for these headphones?

options.A: The cafeteria is open until nine.
options.B: I rolled my ankle on the stairs yesterday.
options.C: Headphones are usually on aisle six.
options.D: Customer service is just past the registers on the right.

items.explanation: 说话人用习语 "get the ball rolling" 询问退货流程的起点。D 直接给出客服位置，正确回应 Where 问题并解决意图。A 是 off_topic。B 抓住 "rolling" 做习语字面误解，polysemy。C 描述耳机货架位置而非退货起点，semantic_association_trap。

items.situation: student checking bus or shuttle schedule
items.speaker: Do you know if the campus shuttle still runs after eleven on weeknights?

options.A: Actually, the last weeknight shuttle leaves at ten-thirty now.
options.B: The shuttle has new plastic seats this semester.
options.C: Yes, I rode the shuttle to the airport once.
options.D: It depends on whether your class is on the east side.

items.explanation: 说话人问深夜班车是否还有，A 用 "Actually" 标记纠正并给出确切时间。B 描述座椅，semantic_association_trap。C 是不相关的过往事件，off_topic。D 把问题误读成日常通勤偏好，wrong_question_type。

items.situation: acknowledging someone's unavailability or schedule conflict
items.speaker: Why does it feel like every Friday lately you're booked solid?

options.A: Yes, Fridays have twenty-four hours.
options.B: Honestly, I've been picking up extra shifts to save for the summer trip.
options.C: The library is busiest on Fridays.
options.D: I haven't been to the bookstore this month.

items.explanation: 说话人问最近周五为何都被占满，B 用 "Honestly" 标记并解释加班存钱的原因，正面回应 Why。A 是 wrong_question_type，把问题当事实查询。C 是 off_topic 语义联想。D 与提问无关，off_topic。

items.situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline
items.speaker: Have you started the draft for our group project that's due Friday?

options.A: I usually print on heavy paper for projects.
options.B: Yes, the library closes at midnight all week.
options.C: Just the introduction so far, but I can send what I have tonight.
options.D: No, I didn't get the email about the new instructor.

items.explanation: 说话人问对方是否开始写草稿，C 用 "Just" 标记说明已写引言并提出分享，符合 marker_led_indirect。A 描述打印用纸，semantic_association_trap。B 答图书馆时间，off_topic。D 把问题错当成 "did you see the email"，wrong_question_type。

items.situation: roommate discussing weekend plans
items.speaker: I'm thinking of biking out to the lake on Saturday if the weather holds.

options.A: The textbook chapter was about a lake in Canada.
options.B: Yes, lakes form when rivers slow down.
options.C: I haven't ridden my bike since last summer.
options.D: That sounds great — count me in if I can borrow a helmet.

items.explanation: 说话人提出周六骑车出游计划。D 接受邀请并提出借头盔的具体跟进，是 direct_topical 回应。A 是 semantic_association_trap，抓住 lake 转向课文。B 是 wrong_question_type，把陈述当地理问答。C 表达个人状态，未回应邀请，off_topic。

items.situation: student checking bus or shuttle schedule
items.speaker: I can't quite remember when the late shuttle stops running.

options.A: Actually, I think the last one leaves around eleven.
options.B: The shuttle stops at the main library.
options.C: Yes, the schedule is posted online.
options.D: I prefer biking around campus anyway.

items.explanation: 正确答案用 marker "Actually" 引导间接回应，给出晚班车的大致末班时间。B 借词 "shuttle" 但只回答地点，C 答非所问，D 完全偏题。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: I'm leaving for the airport at five tomorrow morning.

options.A: The airport has a new food court inside.
options.B: Well, I can drive you if you'd like.
options.C: Flights to Chicago are often delayed lately.
options.D: Yes, I packed everything last night.

items.explanation: 正确答案用 "Well" 引出实际帮助：提供接送，匹配 marker_led_indirect 范式。A 借 "airport" 偏向描述设施，C 与说话人飞行信息无关，D 答非所问。

items.situation: acknowledging someone's unavailability or schedule conflict
items.speaker: How can we meet if you're booked every evening?

options.A: I attended that meeting last week too.
options.B: Yes, my calendar is very full lately.
options.C: How about Saturday morning instead?
options.D: The library has private study rooms available.

items.explanation: 正确答案以反问推进对话，提议周六上午这一替代时间。A 完全偏题，B 答 yes/no 但不推进，D 提供场地但回避时间问题。

items.situation: friend sharing news or personal update
items.speaker: What do you think? I finally got the scholarship.

options.A: I haven't finished the application yet.
options.B: Scholarships usually require an essay.
options.C: The university sends decisions by email.
options.D: That's fantastic news — congratulations!

items.explanation: 正确答案用习惯性的祝贺表达回应好消息。A 偏向自己的情况，B 借 "scholarship" 但说事实，C 与朋友的好消息无关。

items.situation: student discussing a presentation topic with partner
items.speaker: Are you okay if we narrow our presentation to just two examples?

options.A: Actually, that would make our timing much easier.
options.B: Presentations should be at least ten minutes long.
options.C: I missed the last group meeting unfortunately.
options.D: Two students dropped the course last week.

items.explanation: 正确答案以 "Actually" 引导间接同意并指出实际好处。B 与话题相关但答非所问，C 偏题，D 借 "two" 但完全不相关。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Why is this journal not showing up in the catalog search?

options.A: Books on that floor were rearranged last week.
options.B: It's currently being recataloged after a system upgrade.
options.C: The library closes earlier on Saturdays.
options.D: I think the printer is broken again.

items.explanation: 正确答案直接解释 journal 在重新编目所以暂时搜不到。A 借 "floor" 偏题，C 谈关门时间不答问题，D 完全偏题。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: Have you decided when your office hours will move?

options.A: Office hours are usually held on Wednesdays.
options.B: Yes, the policy was approved last term.
options.C: Just email me your questions; I'll respond by tonight.
options.D: The office is on the third floor of Mason Hall.

items.explanation: 正确答案不正面回答时间问题，而是解决学生真正的需求——通过邮件回应问题（context_shift）。A 描述常规情况，B 答非所问，D 谈地点。

items.situation: student checking lost-and-found at front desk
items.speaker: Where can I track down a notebook I left in the lounge?

options.A: Notebooks are sold in the campus store.
options.B: I haven't visited the lounge in weeks.
options.C: The lounge has new study tables this semester.
options.D: You've come to the right place — let's take a look.

items.explanation: 正确答案是常见的习惯性礼貌回应 "You've come to the right place"，表示接下来会帮忙找。A、C 借词偏题，B 答非所问。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Why is this database missing from the search results?

options.A: Actually, that's only available through the new portal.
options.B: Yes, I read several articles from it last week.
options.C: The library opens at eight every morning.
options.D: It contains thousands of recent journal articles.

items.explanation: 正确答案 A 用 marker（Actually）引出间接信息，解释了搜索结果中找不到的原因。B 直接回答“是”并补充无关信息，对“why”是错位回答；C 是营业时间，与问题无关；D 描述数据库内容，未解释为何缺失。

items.situation: student discussing a presentation topic with partner
items.speaker: Are you fine with cutting the second case study?

options.A: I'm fine with the cafeteria menu changes.
options.B: What if we kept it but trimmed it a bit?
options.C: Yes, I always finish my reading on time.
options.D: The second one was hard to find online.

items.explanation: 正确答案 B 用反问推进对话，提出折中方案。A 把 fine 误用为食堂相关内容；C 直接回答 yes 但接无关信息；D 谈案例的可获取性，未回应是否同意删除。

items.situation: calling to check business hours
items.speaker: What hours are you open on the weekend?

options.A: I think the manager hires part-time staff.
options.B: The store is on the corner of Elm.
options.C: Sorry, we're closed for the long weekend.
options.D: Yes, weekends are when I do my shopping.

items.explanation: 正确答案 C 用习惯表达直接说明情况——周末关门，间接回答了营业时间问题。A 谈招聘，与时间无关；B 谈地址，与时间无关；D 直接回答 yes 但答非所问。

items.situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: The radiator in my room has been knocking all week.

options.A: Yes, the dining hall closes at nine tonight.
options.B: The radio in the lounge plays old jazz.
options.C: Most rooms have new energy-efficient windows.
options.D: Please file a maintenance request at the desk.

items.explanation: 正确答案 D 没有直接讨论敲击声，而是转向解决问题的途径，符合 context_shift 模式。A 与暖气无关；B 把 radiator 与 radio 谐音混淆；C 谈窗户但未回应暖气问题。

items.situation: making or rescheduling an appointment
items.speaker: How long does the cleaning appointment usually take?

options.A: Actually, it depends on how busy we are.
options.B: Yes, the appointment has been confirmed already.
options.C: The waiting room is just past the front desk.
options.D: We provide complimentary tea during your visit.

items.explanation: 正确答案 A 用 Actually 引出间接回答，说明时长不固定。B 直接回答 yes 但答非所问；C 谈地点；D 谈附加服务。

items.situation: roommate discussing weekend plans
items.speaker: Where did you end up going for brunch?

options.A: Yes, brunch ran longer than I expected.
options.B: Just that little place near the bookstore.
options.C: I made pancakes for everyone last weekend.
options.D: Brunch is my favorite meal of the day.

items.explanation: 正确答案 B 用 Just 引出间接但确切的地点回答。A 直接回答 yes 但答非所问；C 谈做饭，地点不对；D 谈喜好，未回答地点。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: I might leave for the airport around four.

options.A: Yes, airports are pretty busy these days.
options.B: My flight last month was delayed two hours.
options.C: Want me to drive you and split the gas?
options.D: I always pack my suitcase the night before.

items.explanation: 正确答案 C 用反问推进对话，提议拼车，回应了对方的计划。A 直接回答 yes 但答非所问；B 谈过往航班，与计划无关；D 谈打包习惯，与请求无关。

items.situation: acknowledging someone's unavailability or schedule conflict
items.speaker: Are you sure you can't make it on Friday?

options.A: Yes, the project deadline is on Friday.
options.B: Friday is when the new store opens.
options.C: I usually go running on Friday evenings.
options.D: Actually, I might be able to swing by briefly.

items.explanation: 正确答案 D 用 Actually 引出间接回应，表明可能可以短暂赴约。A 直接回答 yes 但增加无关信息；B 谈新店开张，与是否赴约无关；C 谈跑步习惯，与是否赴约无关。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: Where's all that hammering coming from this morning?

options.A: I'll go ask them to wrap it up soon.
options.B: Hammers are stored in the basement closet.
options.C: The morning light hits that window perfectly.
options.D: He fixed the fence last weekend already.

items.explanation: 正确答案 A 体现 context_shift 范式：没有直接回答"在哪里"，而是直接解决说话人潜在的不满——主动去叫对方停下来。B 是语义联想陷阱（hammering → hammers 工具存放位置），与说话人想知道噪音来源无关。C 是离题选项（用 morning 这个词造一个无关的句子）。D 是答错问题类型（说话人问的是 where，D 回答的是 when 和 what，且时间不对）。

items.situation: checking if a service (post office, bank) is open
items.speaker: I really need to mail this package before five.

options.A: Stamps come in books of twenty now.
options.B: Actually, the post office stays open till six tonight.
options.C: He sent the letter from Boston last week.
options.D: Yes, you can ship it however you like.

items.explanation: 正确答案 B 以话语标记 Actually 开头，间接回应说话人的担忧——告诉对方时间还来得及。A 是语义联想陷阱（mail → stamps 邮票），没有回应说话人的时间压力。C 是离题但语法正确的句子（不同的人、不同的时间、不同的城市）。D 是答错问题类型（说话人是陈述句表达担忧，D 却给出 yes/no 式许可，逻辑错位）。

items.situation: student checking bus or shuttle schedule
items.speaker: Why does the shuttle skip the south lot on Sundays?

options.A: The shuttle is painted blue and white.
options.B: Yes, the schedule changed last semester.
options.C: Are you trying to get to the dorms by noon?
options.D: Sunday brunch is served until two.

items.explanation: 正确答案 C 体现 counter_question 范式：用一个推进对话的反问，弄清说话人真正的需求，从而能给出有用建议。A 是语义联想陷阱（shuttle → 描述班车外观），完全没回应问题。B 是答错问题类型（说话人问 why，B 用 yes 开头答非所问）。D 是离题选项（借用 Sunday 这个词造无关的句子）。

items.situation: student discussing a presentation topic with partner
items.speaker: What angle should we take on the climate paper?

options.A: Climate is one of the required units this term.
options.B: I turned in my draft on Tuesday morning.
options.C: Yes, that section was pretty long.
options.D: How about focusing on coastal cities?

items.explanation: 正确答案 D 体现 counter_question 范式：用一个具体的提议性反问推进讨论，给出可行的切入点。A 是语义联想陷阱（climate → 关于气候课程的事实陈述），没有回答如何取角度。B 是离题但语法正确（说自己交了草稿，与角度选择无关）。C 是答错问题类型（说话人问 what angle，C 却用 yes 回应，逻辑不通）。

items.situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed
items.speaker: You look completely wiped out today.

options.A: Want me to grab you a coffee?
options.B: The towel is hanging by the sink.
options.C: I cleaned the whole kitchen last night.
options.D: No, that movie wasn't very good.

items.explanation: 正确答案 A 体现 counter_question 范式：不直接评论对方的状态，而是用一个体贴的反问主动提供帮助，自然推进对话。B 是语义联想陷阱（wiped → wipe/towel 抹布毛巾，利用一词多义）。C 是离题选项（说自己昨晚做了什么，与对方的疲惫无关）。D 是答错问题类型（说话人是关切的陈述，D 却用 no 回应一个根本没问的问题）。

items.situation: friend sharing news or personal update
items.speaker: How did your sister's wedding go last weekend?

options.A: Weddings are usually held in the spring.
options.B: It was beautiful — everyone had a great time.
options.C: She lives in Chicago with her husband now.
options.D: Where did you find that recipe again?

items.explanation: 正确答案 B 体现 direct_topical 范式：直接、温暖地回答说话人问的婚礼怎么样。A 是语义联想陷阱（wedding → 关于婚礼季节的一般性事实，没回答这一场）。C 是离题选项（讲妹妹现在住哪里，没回答婚礼本身怎么样）。D 是答错问题类型（说话人问 how，D 反问 where 完全转换话题）。

items.situation: making or rescheduling an appointment
items.speaker: Can we push my haircut to Thursday afternoon?

options.A: The salon redecorated the front lobby.
options.B: Yes, I trimmed mine just last month.
options.C: Actually, Friday morning would work better for us.
options.D: When did the new stylist start working?

items.explanation: 正确答案 C 以话语标记 Actually 开头，间接回应预约请求——提出一个更可行的替代时间。A 是语义联想陷阱（haircut → salon 美发店装修），与改约时间无关。B 是答错问题类型/误解（把对方的预约请求理解成了关于剪头发的一般经验）。D 是离题选项（反问新发型师入职时间，完全偏离改约这件事）。

items.situation: student asking about a reading list for a course
items.speaker: Did the professor finalize the reading list yet?

options.A: Reading rooms close at midnight on weekdays.
options.B: She lectured for almost two hours yesterday.
options.C: I finished the novel over the break.
options.D: Your guess is as good as mine.

items.explanation: 正确答案 D 体现 idiomatic 范式：用地道的英语习语 "Your guess is as good as mine"（我也不知道，跟你一样不清楚）自然回应。A 是语义联想陷阱（reading → reading rooms 阅览室开放时间）。B 是离题选项（讲教授昨天讲课多久，没回答阅读清单是否定下来）。C 是误解/答错问题类型（把 reading list 误解成具体的小说阅读经历）。

items.situation: acknowledging someone's unavailability or schedule conflict
items.speaker: Why didn't you make it to the meeting?

options.A: Long story — I'll tell you over coffee.
options.B: The meeting was very informative this time.
options.C: I'll definitely attend next week's meeting.
options.D: Most people did show up on time.

items.explanation: A 是地道的回避式回答，暗示有复杂原因但不在此说。B 答非所问，说会议内容。C 是承诺未来出席，不回答缺席原因。D 离题，谈他人。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: Where should I include the raw data table?

options.A: Make sure your calculations are clearly shown.
options.B: Stick it in an appendix at the end.
options.C: Yes, raw data is important to include.
options.D: Tables are usually clearer than long paragraphs.

items.explanation: B 用地道短语 stick it in 指明附在附录。A 答非所问，谈计算。C 是 yes/no 形式，但问题是 where 不是 whether。D 是离题的一般评论。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Do you carry any books on Persian poetry?

options.A: The poetry section is on the third floor.
options.B: Persian literature is fascinating to read about.
options.C: Absolutely — let me show you the section.
options.D: You should also try the music collection.

items.explanation: C 是地道直接回应，'Absolutely' 表达肯定并主动带路。A 给位置但不回答是否有。B 离题评论。D 是错误推荐。

items.situation: coordinating airport pickup with a friend
items.speaker: How long will it take to get to the airport?

options.A: Yes, I can pick you up on Friday.
options.B: The flight arrives at six in the morning.
options.C: The terminal closest to us is Terminal C.
options.D: Forty minutes if traffic isn't bad.

items.explanation: D 直接回应时长，条件句很地道。A 是 yes/no 形式不匹配。B 谈航班时间，不答行程时长。C 谈航站楼位置，离题。

items.situation: student asking about course enrollment or prerequisites
items.speaker: I'm worried about taking the advanced statistics course.

options.A: Actually, the intro course covers everything you'll need.
options.B: The advanced course is offered every spring semester.
options.C: Statistics is one of the most popular majors here.
options.D: Many students worry about hard courses initially.

items.explanation: A 以 'Actually' 开头并间接化解担忧。B 给出无关排程信息。C 谈专业受欢迎度，离题。D 是泛泛的安慰但没给解决方案。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: Can I come by your office tomorrow?

options.A: My office is in the new building.
options.B: Just check the door — I'm there all afternoon.
options.C: Yes, office visits are encouraged regularly.
options.D: Tomorrow's class is being held online.

items.explanation: B 以 'Just' 开头，间接答允许并告知何时。A 给位置但不回答是否方便。C 形式上 yes 但模糊，没指明何时。D 完全离题，谈线上课。

items.situation: student asking about campus shuttle or parking
items.speaker: The shuttle is running late again today.

options.A: Buses are the most efficient transportation option.
options.B: The shuttle stops at five locations on campus.
options.C: Maybe walking would be faster at this point.
options.D: Today's weather has been unusually warm.

items.explanation: C 以 'Maybe' 提出实际替代方案，是地道间接回应。A 是泛泛评论。B 离题谈站点数量。D 与对话无关。
