items.situation: student asking about a campus event or concert
items.speaker: What time does the spring concert start?

options.A: Yes, I'm planning to go this year too.
options.B: Concerts are usually held in the main hall.
options.C: Tickets are available at the box office.
options.D: Did you see the flyer near the entrance?

items.explanation: D 用反问引导对方查看海报，是自然的对话推进。A 是 yes/no 形式不对。B 谈场地。C 谈票，都不回答时间问题。

items.situation: student asking about a reading list for a course
items.speaker: I still haven't found the reading list for History 220.

options.A: It should be on the course portal.
options.B: Yes, the library is open until midnight.
options.C: I love the historical fiction section.
options.D: We meet every Tuesday and Thursday.

items.explanation: 对方在说找不到 reading list，自然回应是指出获取路径。A 正确。B 答非所问（开门时间）；C 离题；D 是关于上课时间的联想陷阱。

items.situation: student asking about gym or sports facility hours
items.speaker: Why does the pool close at noon on Saturdays?

options.A: It's just down the hall from the locker rooms.
options.B: They reserve afternoons for swim team training.
options.C: I love swimming early in the morning.
options.D: Yes, you should bring a towel.

items.explanation: 对方在问 Why 关闭，B 给出真正原因（swim team 训练）。A 回答地点，错问题类型；C 离题；D 是 swim 联想陷阱。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Do you know if the library has the Tomasello book on shared intentionality?

options.A: Yes, it's a fascinating cognitive science topic.
options.B: No, I haven't read it yet myself.
options.C: Try the inter-library loan if needed.
options.D: The reference desk closes at nine.

items.explanation: 对方在找一本书，C 直接给出解决方案（如果本馆没有，去馆际互借）。A 把焦点转到话题趣味，是 cog sci 联想；B 答非所问；D 离题。

items.situation: student asking about printing or scanning at computer lab
items.speaker: How do I print double-sided in the computer lab?

options.A: Yes, the lab is open until eleven.
options.B: I usually scan documents at the front desk.
options.C: The scanner accepts USB sticks too.
options.D: Check the duplex box before you print.

items.explanation: D 直接给出操作步骤（勾 duplex），回答 How 问题。A 是 yes/no，错问题类型；B 偏到 scan；C 是 scanner 联想。

items.situation: checking if a service is still open
items.speaker: Is the post office at the student union still open?

options.A: I think they close at five today.
options.B: No, I haven't received any packages yet.
options.C: The union sells the best smoothies on campus.
options.D: Yes, it's a great place to study together.

items.explanation: A 间接回答"是否还开门"，告知关门时间。B 答非所问（包裹）；C 离题（smoothies）；D 把 union 当成自习空间联想。

items.situation: calling to check business hours
items.speaker: What time does the bookshop close on weekends?

options.A: Yes, they sell secondhand chemistry texts.
options.B: Their website probably lists the holiday hours.
options.C: I picked up a great cookbook there last week.
options.D: The mall stays open until eleven on Saturdays.

items.explanation: B 给出查询途径（看网站），间接回应 What time。A 是 yes/no 错类型；C 离题；D 是相邻 mall 联想陷阱。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: Where am I supposed to upload the chemistry lab report?

options.A: Yes, the deadline is tonight at midnight.
options.B: I borrowed the lab manual from a classmate.
options.C: Did your TA share the new link?
options.D: The chemistry building is on the south side.

items.explanation: C 用反问澄清前提（TA 是否发过新链接），属于 counter_question 套路。A 是 yes/no 错类型；B 偏题；D 是 chemistry 物理位置联想。

items.situation: student discussing a presentation topic with partner
items.speaker: I'm completely drawing a blank for our project topic.

options.A: The presentation is worth thirty percent of our grade.
options.B: Yes, I love drawing maps for our slides.
options.C: The library has new whiteboards on the second floor.
options.D: Let's grab coffee and brainstorm together this afternoon.

items.explanation: "Drawing a blank" 是想不出主意的习语，D 提议一起头脑风暴，正面回应。A 离题；B 把 drawing 字面理解为画画；C 是"whiteboard/blank"联想陷阱。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's situation
items.speaker: How are you holding up after the move?

options.A: Honestly, still unpacking boxes everywhere.
options.B: I really enjoyed the weather yesterday.
options.C: The library is open until eleven.
options.D: Yes, I know several people there.

items.explanation: 正确答案以"Honestly"开头并间接回应搬家后的状态，符合marker_led_indirect模式。B话题完全无关；C回答地点而非状况；D是知人不是知状态，属polysemy陷阱。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: Why didn't we have to include raw data this time?

options.A: I haven't seen the data file yet either.
options.B: Beats me, that part wasn't on the rubric.
options.C: The lab is on the second floor.
options.D: Yes, raw data is important to include.

items.explanation: 正确答案使用习语"Beats me"承认不知道并补充说明，符合idiomatic paradigm。A是模糊共情，是经典的"shares the speaker's concern"陷阱；C回答地点；D矛盾且明显错误。

items.situation: making or rescheduling an appointment
items.speaker: Where should we meet for the consultation?

options.A: I'm free Tuesday morning, actually.
options.B: Yes, I know that coffee shop.
options.C: How about the lobby of City Hall?
options.D: The consultation took about an hour.

items.explanation: 正确答案用反问"How about..."提出地点建议，推进对话，符合counter_question paradigm。A回答时间而非地点；B只是认知陷阱；D谈时长，话题已过。

items.situation: asking a store clerk about a return or exchange
items.speaker: I'd like to exchange this jacket for a smaller size.

options.A: The jacket section is upstairs by escalators.
options.B: Yes, I bought it here last week.
options.C: I returned my coat just two days ago.
options.D: Of course, can I see your receipt?

items.explanation: 正确答案以"Of course"开头并自然推进交易（要收据），符合idiomatic paradigm。A回答地点而非业务；B无关；C是和店员无关的另一人经历。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Do you have any books on Renaissance music theory?

options.A: Absolutely, follow me to the music section.
options.B: I read a music history book yesterday.
options.C: The library closes at midnight tonight.
options.D: Yes, I love Renaissance paintings too.

items.explanation: 正确答案用习语"Absolutely"承诺帮助并引导到位置，符合idiomatic paradigm。B是别人经历；C回答时间；D偷换主题到paintings。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: Can you pick me up before the meeting?

options.A: The meeting will start at three sharp.
options.B: What time should I be at your place?
options.C: Yes, I drove to the meeting last time.
options.D: I left my keys on the table.

items.explanation: 正确答案用反问推进时间细节，符合counter_question paradigm。A回答会议时间而非接送；C回答历史而非未来；D无关。

items.situation: student asking about campus shuttle or parking
items.speaker: What time does the last shuttle leave campus?

options.A: The shuttle is usually packed in mornings.
options.B: Yes, I take the shuttle pretty often.
options.C: It departs at eleven o'clock sharp.
options.D: The stop is by the science building.

items.explanation: 正确答案直接回答时间问题，符合罕见的direct_topical paradigm。A谈人多而非时间；B只是肯定坐班车；D回答地点。

items.situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: My shower has been draining slowly all week.

options.A: The dorm has new water heaters installed.
options.B: Yes, I had a slow drain last semester.
options.C: I usually shower in the morning anyway.
options.D: Did you submit a maintenance request online?

items.explanation: 正确答案用反问推进解决方案，符合counter_question paradigm。A切换到无关话题；B是"我也有过"的共情陷阱；C转向无关的个人习惯。

items.situation: coordinating airport pickup with a friend
items.speaker: I won't be able to pick up Megan from the airport tomorrow.

options.A: No worries, I'll grab her instead.
options.B: Megan said she prefers a window seat.
options.C: The airport food court has improved a lot.
options.D: Yes, the flight is right on schedule.

items.explanation: 说话者表达自己无法去机场接Megan，最合适的回应是主动接替任务而不是直接回应字面意思。B、C是语义联想陷阱（机场→飞机/机场设施）；D是答非所问。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: Where did you save your notes from yesterday's economics lecture?

options.A: Economics has become my favorite course this term.
options.B: I uploaded them to the shared course folder this morning.
options.C: Sure, I would love to grab coffee later today.
options.D: No, I haven't taken that class yet myself.

items.explanation: 对方问笔记保存在哪里，最佳回应是直接说明上传位置。A是话题联想陷阱（经济学话题）；C是答非所问；D答错了问题类型。

items.situation: student asking about a campus event or concert
items.speaker: What night does the jazz band perform at the student center?

options.A: Yes, the student center is open until midnight.
options.B: The jazz band has been around for ten years.
options.C: I think they go on at eight on Friday.
options.D: Sure, I'd be happy to join you for dinner.

items.explanation: 对方问演出时间，最佳回应是给出具体时间。A答错了问题类型；B是关于乐队的偏题信息；D是答非所问。

items.situation: friend inviting another to a study group or social event
items.speaker: How about joining our study group for biology this Thursday evening?

options.A: Biology is my hardest class this semester.
options.B: Yes, I went to a great concert last Thursday.
options.C: She thinks the textbook is overpriced this year.
options.D: Sure, what time were you planning to meet?

items.explanation: 对方邀请加入学习小组，最自然的回应是接受并问细节。A是语义联想陷阱；B是偏题；C是话题转移。

items.situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed
items.speaker: Why do you look so overwhelmed right now?

options.A: My thesis draft is due first thing in the morning.
options.B: Yes, the cafeteria gets busy around this time.
options.C: I usually study in the library on weekends.
options.D: No, I have not seen him since yesterday.

items.explanation: 对方问为什么显得不堪重负，最佳回应是说明压力的具体原因。B答错了问题类型；C偏离话题；D是答非所问。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: Do we need to include raw data tables in this week's lab report?

options.A: Yes, the laboratory closes at six on weekdays.
options.B: Which experiment are you asking about exactly?
options.C: The new microscopes were finally delivered.
options.D: I bought a binder for my report last week.

items.explanation: TA以反问明确所指实验，是符合实际场景的回应。A答错问题；C是话题联想陷阱；D是偏题。

items.situation: asking a store clerk about a return or exchange
items.speaker: Can I exchange this sweater for a different size?

options.A: Yes, sweaters are usually heavier in the winter.
options.B: The store closed for renovations last weekend.
options.C: Sure, do you have your original receipt with you?
options.D: I prefer cotton blends for everyday clothing.

items.explanation: 店员通过追问收据来回应换货请求，是合理且自然的回应。A是语义联想陷阱；B是偏题；D是话题转移。

items.situation: student asking about printing or scanning at computer lab
items.speaker: I'm at my wits' end trying to figure out the new scanning station.

options.A: I'll buy more printer ink tomorrow afternoon.
options.B: Scanning is faster than printing these days.
options.C: The lab has been open since seven this morning.
options.D: Let me show you the ropes — it took me a while too.

items.explanation: 说话者用习语"at my wits' end"表示束手无策，D用习语"show you the ropes"提供帮助是最自然的回应。A、B是语义联想陷阱；C是偏题。

items.situation: student asking about a blocked parking lot
items.speaker: Why's the south parking lot blocked off this morning?

options.A: The west lot has open spaces today.
options.B: Yes, I parked there myself yesterday.
options.C: I think the gates open at seven.
options.D: It's been blocked since last semester.

items.explanation: A采用context_shift——不解释原因而直接帮助解决停车问题，符合自然对话。B是yes/no错配；C回答时间而非原因；D将临时封闭说成长期封闭，事实错误。

items.situation: student asking about midterm exam format
items.speaker: How's the new exam format this year?

options.A: The library has every old exam on file.
options.B: As a matter of fact, it's almost identical.
options.C: I'll be taking it on Monday morning.
options.D: Yes, the format definitely matters for prep.

items.explanation: B使用习语'As a matter of fact'引出对格式的直接比较，符合idiomatic范式。A答非所问；C讲自己什么时候考试；D是yes/no错配。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report graph requirements
items.speaker: Aren't graphs required in this week's lab report?

options.A: I usually use blue ink for my graphs.
options.B: The report should be at least four pages.
options.C: Do you mean from the calibration data or the trial?
options.D: Yes, the lab opens at eight on weekends.

items.explanation: C用counter_question要求澄清是哪一组数据的图，是助教自然的反问，符合范式。A关于书写工具；B关于篇幅而非图表；D是错配yes/no答非所问。

items.situation: two friends deciding where to get lunch
items.speaker: I'm really craving something hot and spicy.

options.A: The student cafeteria opens at eleven on weekdays.
options.B: Sure, I had a quick sandwich earlier today.
options.C: Yeah, I think those reviews are mostly positive.
options.D: Absolutely, let's hit that Thai place on Maple.

items.explanation: D使用习语'Absolutely'+'let's hit'积极响应辛辣食物的偏好，建议去Thai餐馆，符合idiomatic范式。A答非所问；B描述自己已吃过；C关于评价不对应美食选择。

items.situation: roommate sharing quiet weekend plans
items.speaker: I'm probably staying in to catch up on reading.

options.A: Actually, count me in — I need to study too.
options.B: The bookstore is closing early on Saturday.
options.C: I read three chapters last weekend.
options.D: Yes, the library is usually quiet then.

items.explanation: A以discourse marker'Actually'开头并间接表态加入对方的安静计划，符合marker_led_indirect范式。B、C、D都答非所问，只重复reading这个语义关联词。

items.situation: customer asking a clerk where to leave a returned bag
items.speaker: Where's the right place to leave a returned bag?

options.A: I bought my own jacket online last week.
options.B: Just take it to the counter near fitting rooms.
options.C: The store usually closes at nine on Sunday.
options.D: Yes, all returned bags must be sealed first.

items.explanation: B用习语副词'Just'引出明确而口语化的指引，符合idiomatic范式。A答非所问讲购物方式；C讲营业时间；D是yes/no错配。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about books on Roman engineering
items.speaker: Aren't there books on Roman aqueduct engineering here?

options.A: Yes, the Roman art exhibit closes next week.
options.B: We open at eight on weekday mornings only.
options.C: Yes, the engineering section has several recent ones.
options.D: I read about aqueducts in high school years.

items.explanation: C直接回应可借到的书所在区域，是direct_topical范式的恰当回答。A借用Roman做语义陷阱但答非所问；B回答时间；D讲个人经历。

items.situation: acknowledging a Thursday volunteer schedule conflict
items.speaker: What's our plan for Thursday's volunteer slot?

options.A: Yes, every Thursday is usually pretty busy.
options.B: The community garden is over on Maple Street.
options.C: It typically starts at four every single week.
options.D: Could you swap with Lily for Friday's morning shift?

items.explanation: D用counter_question提出具体解决方案——和Lily调班，是推进对话的反问，符合counter_question范式。A是yes/no错配；B回答地点；C回答时间。

items.situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline
items.speaker: Do you think we can finish the project by Friday?

options.A: Friday is the last day of classes.
options.B: Why don't we ask the professor for an extension?
options.C: The project has to include a bibliography.
options.D: I think I finished my part already.

items.B: Why don't we ask the professor for an extension?
items.explanation: 正确答案是B。说话人担心能否按时完成，B选项提出向教授申请延期，直接解决了时间压力问题，是context_shift。A选项是off_topic，只陈述了周五是最后一天上课，没有帮助。C选项是semantic_association_trap，由'project'联想到'bibliography'，但无关紧要。D选项是wrong_question_type，回答的是'是否完成自己的部分'，而不是'能否完成整个项目'。

items.situation: student discussing a presentation topic with partner
items.speaker: I'm worried our presentation topic is too broad.

options.A: The presentation is next Tuesday.
options.B: I think the professor likes broad topics.
options.C: Well, we could focus on just one aspect.
options.D: The topic was assigned by the professor.

items.explanation: 正确答案是C。C以话语标记'Well'开头，提出缩小范围的实际建议，是marker_led_indirect。A选项是off_topic，只说了演示日期，没有回应担心。B选项是semantic_association_trap，由'presentation'和'professor'联想到教授喜好，但并非事实。D选项是off_topic，陈述话题来源，无助于解决问题。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: Could you please keep the music down after 10 PM?

options.A: Absolutely, I'll turn it down.
options.B: The music is my favorite band.
options.C: I usually go to bed at 11 PM.
options.D: The walls are very thin in this building.

items.explanation: 正确答案是A。'Absolutely'是自然习语，表示爽快同意，符合idiomatic范式。B选项是semantic_association_trap，由'music'联想到'band'，但无关。C选项是off_topic，说自己睡觉时间，没有回应请求。D选项是off_topic，抱怨墙薄，没有同意或拒绝。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: Professor, when is your office hours this week?

options.A: The assignment is due next Friday.
options.B: What time works best for you?
options.C: I have a meeting at 3 PM.
options.D: Office hours are usually in the library.

items.explanation: 正确答案是B。B以反问回应，询问对方方便时间，是counter_question，推进对话。A选项是semantic_association_trap，由'office hours'联想到'assignment'，但无关。C选项是off_topic，说自己有会议，没有回答。D选项是wrong_question_type，回答了'办公室时间在哪里'，而不是'什么时候'。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: Could I borrow your notes from yesterday's history lecture?

options.A: The history lecture was very long.
options.B: I need to study for the exam too.
options.C: The notes are in my locker right now.
options.D: Sure, I'll send you a copy later.

items.explanation: 正确答案是D。D直接同意并承诺发送，是direct_topical。A选项是semantic_association_trap，由'lecture'联想到'long'，但无关。B选项是off_topic，说自己也要复习，没有回应请求。C选项是off_topic，说笔记在储物柜，但没有同意或拒绝借用。

items.situation: checking if a service (post office, bank) is open
items.speaker: Do you know if the post office is still open?

options.A: I think it closes at five today.
options.B: I need to buy some stamps.
options.C: The mail came an hour ago.
options.D: Yes, I know the postman.

items.explanation: 正确答案A直接回答对方的问题，提供了邮局关门时间的信息，是典型的直接事实回应。B选项说需要买邮票，是语义关联陷阱，因为提到了邮局相关物品，但并未回答是否开门的问题。C选项谈论邮件已送达，与询问营业时间无关，属于离题选项。D选项回答“认识邮递员”，将问题中的“知道”误解为“认识某人”，属于误解陷阱。

items.situation: student checking bus or shuttle schedule
items.speaker: The bus to the library doesn't come for another twenty minutes.

options.A: The library is open until midnight.
options.B: Let's just walk, it's not that far.
options.C: I missed the bus yesterday too.
options.D: Twenty minutes is a long time.

items.explanation: 正确答案B没有直接回应等待时间，而是提出了一个替代方案（走路），解决了对方隐含的“如何到达图书馆”的问题，属于语境转换。A选项谈论图书馆开放时间，与等待公交车无关，是离题选项。C选项说昨天也错过了公交车，虽然关联了“公交车”主题，但并未解决当前困境，是语义关联陷阱。D选项只是重复了时间信息，没有推进对话或提供帮助，不够自然。

items.situation: student seeking help at the writing center
items.speaker: Can you take a look at my thesis statement before I submit it?

options.A: I wrote my thesis last semester.
options.B: I submitted it this morning.
options.C: Sure, I'd be happy to.
options.D: The statement is on the board.

items.explanation: 正确答案C使用了习语式回答“Sure, I'd be happy to”，直接且礼貌地表示愿意帮助，符合请求协助的对话场景。A选项说“我上学期写了论文”，将“thesis”理解为整个论文而非论文陈述，是语义关联陷阱。B选项说“我今早提交了”，混淆了“提交”这个动作的施动者，属于离题。D选项说“陈述在黑板上”，将“statement”字面理解为写在黑板上的文字，是误解陷阱。

items.situation: friend sharing news or personal update
items.speaker: I finally got the job I applied for last month.

options.A: You applied for a job last month?
options.B: I got a new phone last week.
options.C: The job market is tough right now.
options.D: That's awesome, congratulations!

items.explanation: 正确答案D使用习语式表达“That's awesome, congratulations!”，这是对好消息的自然、热情回应，符合社交语境。A选项重复了对方话语中的部分信息，但以疑问形式出现，显得不自然且多余，属于离题。B选项说“我上周买了新手机”，完全转换话题，是离题选项。C选项谈论就业市场现状，虽然相关但过于泛泛，没有直接回应对方的喜讯，属于语义关联陷阱。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: How can I keep my music down when you're studying?

options.A: Could you just use headphones?
options.B: I study in the library mostly.
options.C: The music is too loud.
options.D: I'm not studying right now.

items.explanation: 正确答案A以反问形式提出解决方案（用耳机），既回应了问题又推进了对话，属于反问范式。B选项说“我大多在图书馆学习”，并未直接解决噪音问题，是离题选项。C选项抱怨音乐太大声，但对方已经在询问如何降低音量，重复问题无帮助，属于语义关联陷阱。D选项说“我现在没在学习”，暗示噪音当前没问题，但未解决未来可能的问题，属于误解陷阱。

items.situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: When is maintenance going to fix the broken heater?

options.A: The heater is very old.
options.B: They said someone would come by tomorrow morning.
options.C: I fixed my own heater once.
options.D: Maintenance is closed on weekends.

items.explanation: 正确答案B使用习语式回答，直接提供了维修人员何时来的信息，并带有“他们说”这一自然引述，符合对话预期。A选项描述暖气片老旧，没有回答时间问题，是语义关联陷阱。C选项说“我自己修过一次”，转移话题至个人经历，是离题选项。D选项说维修处周末关门，虽然与维修相关，但并未回答“何时来修”的具体问题，属于回答错误问题类型。

items.situation: student asking about printing or scanning at computer lab
items.speaker: Do you know if this printer does double-sided printing?

options.A: Yes, I printed something yesterday.
options.B: The printer is out of paper.
options.C: Actually, I think you have to set it in the menu.
options.D: I need to print my assignment too.

items.explanation: 正确答案C以话语标记词“Actually”开头，间接提供了如何设置双面打印的信息，符合标记引导的间接范式。A选项回答“我昨天打印了东西”，虽然关联了“打印”，但并未回答双面打印的问题，属于语义关联陷阱。B选项说打印机缺纸，是离题信息，未回应核心问题。D选项说“我也要打印作业”，转移焦点至个人需求，属于离题选项。

items.situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed
items.speaker: Where did I put my keys? I'm going to be late.

options.A: You should calm down first.
options.B: Check your jacket pocket.
options.C: I lost my keys last week.
options.D: The bus leaves in ten minutes.

items.explanation: 正确答案B直接提出了一个具体的寻找钥匙的位置建议，是对问题的直接帮助，属于直接主题回答。A选项建议冷静，虽然合理但未直接解决找钥匙的问题，是离题选项。C选项说“我上周丢了钥匙”，转移话题至个人经历，是语义关联陷阱。D选项说公交车十分钟后出发，增加了时间压力但未帮助找钥匙，属于离题。

items.situation: coordinating airport pickup with a friend
items.speaker: What time does your flight land on Friday?

options.A: Well, it's supposed to arrive around six, but it might be delayed.
options.B: I prefer to fly on weekdays.
options.C: The airport is very busy on Fridays.
options.D: My flight number is UA 123.

items.explanation: 正确答案A以话语标记词“Well”开头，间接提供了落地时间并预判了可能的延误，既回答了问题又提供了额外有用信息，符合标记引导的间接范式。B选项说喜欢工作日飞行，与问题无关，是离题选项。C选项谈论机场繁忙程度，是语义关联陷阱（关联“机场”和“周五”）。D选项提供了航班号，但并未回答落地时间问题，属于回答错误问题类型。

items.situation: staff at the front desk helping a student who lost an ID card
items.speaker: Where do I report a lost student ID?

options.A: The ID photos came out really well.
options.B: I lost my keys somewhere yesterday.
options.C: Right here at this desk works fine.
options.D: Students get a discount at the store.

items.explanation: 正确答案C直接回答了地点问题（就在这个柜台办理），符合direct_topical范式。A是语义联想陷阱（抓住ID联想到证件照效果，答非所问）；B是离题但合乎语法的句子（谈论自己丢钥匙，与挂失学生证地点无关）；D是语义联想陷阱（抓住student联想到学生折扣，完全不切题）。

items.situation: advisor asking whether a student declared a major
items.speaker: Have you decided on a major yet?

options.A: Let's look at your options together.
options.B: The major requirements are listed online.
options.C: Yes, the building is across the quad.
options.D: No, I haven't met my roommate yet.

items.explanation: 正确答案A采用情境转移范式——顾问不纠结于是否已决定的字面回答，而是直接帮学生解决选择难题（我们一起看看你的选项）。B是语义联想陷阱（抓住major谈论专业要求清单，没推进对话）；C是错误问题类型（用Yes回答后谈论建筑位置，答非所问）；D虽以No开头看似合理，却属于错误情境（把问句误解为是否见过室友），离题。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: Are you driving to the conference tomorrow morning?

options.A: Sure thing, I've got a spare seat.
options.B: The conference room is on the third floor.
options.C: I lost my keys somewhere yesterday.
options.D: Drivers must register at the gate.

items.explanation: A用'Sure thing'惯用语并主动提供搭车，正面回应。B是'conference'词陷阱，谈房间位置。C完全跑题。D是'driver'词陷阱。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's situation
items.speaker: You've been so quiet at lunch lately.

options.A: Lunch starts at noon today.
options.B: Actually, I've had a rough week.
options.C: She lives quite far from campus.
options.D: The cafeteria is closed for repairs.

items.explanation: B用'Actually'开头并解释原因，回应朋友的关心。A是'lunch'词陷阱。C是无关话题。D借用'lunch'语境但跑题。

items.situation: student seeking help at the writing center
items.speaker: Why is the writing center so busy today?

options.A: Yes, I went there a few times yesterday.
options.B: The center employs eight tutors in total.
options.C: Well, midterm essays are due Monday.
options.D: I usually prefer the library for studying.

items.explanation: C用'Well'开头解释原因——期中论文要交。A答错了问题类型（why问原因，答了yes/no）。B借用'center'但答了不同方面。D完全跑题。

items.situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: What should I do about my broken heater?

options.A: The dorm has three different heaters total.
options.B: Yes, the cold spell starts tomorrow.
options.C: I bought a small space heater online.
options.D: Just call the maintenance hotline.

items.explanation: D用'Just'引导给出实用建议——直接打维修电话。A是'heater'词陷阱。B答了yes/no而非what。C用'heater'但跑题。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours
items.speaker: Are your office hours still on Thursday?

options.A: Actually, I moved them to Friday.
options.B: The office is on the second floor.
options.C: Yes, I always teach on Mondays.
options.D: Library hours extend until midnight tonight.

items.explanation: A用'Actually'开头并间接更正——办公时间已改到周五。B是'office'词陷阱，答位置。C答yes但说错信息。D是'hours'词陷阱。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: I'm not sure how to format the data tables.

options.A: Tables are usually rectangular in shape.
options.B: Have you checked the sample report online?
options.C: Reports are due by Friday at noon.
options.D: Data shows we improved this semester.

items.explanation: B以反问推进对话，引导学生去看示范。A是'table'多义陷阱。C是'report'词陷阱，答时间不答格式。D是'data'词陷阱。

items.situation: friend inviting another to a study group or social event
items.speaker: How about coffee before the study group?

options.A: Coffee was discovered centuries ago by accident.
options.B: Yes, I drink coffee every single morning.
options.C: Sounds perfect, let's meet at six.
options.D: Groups must register at the front desk.

items.explanation: C用'Sounds perfect'惯用语接受邀请并约时间。A是'coffee'词陷阱跑题。B答yes但与提议无关。D是'group'词陷阱。

items.situation: student asking a professor about an assignment extension
items.speaker: Did you get my email about the assignment extension?

options.A: Email is faster than regular mail.
options.B: Yes, the assignments are due next week.
options.C: Office hours are on Wednesdays this term.
options.D: Actually, I responded this morning.

items.explanation: D用'Actually'开头间接确认收到邮件并已回复。A是'email'词陷阱。B借用'assignment'答了截止日期但与邮件无关。C完全跑题。

items.situation: Asking about a chapter assignment
items.speaker: Do you know which chapters Professor Lin assigned for Friday?

options.A: Chapters four and five, plus the practice problems.
options.B: Friday is my favorite day of the week.
options.C: I'll get a new copy of the textbook tomorrow.
options.D: She is one of the best lecturers in the department.

items.explanation: A 直接给出章节信息。B 与日期相关但无意义。C 谈买教材，与所问无关。D 评价教授本人，未回答问题。

items.situation: Friend invites to grab dinner
items.speaker: Want to grab dinner at the new ramen place tonight?

options.A: I had a huge lunch and I'm still pretty full.
options.B: The ramen there comes with extra noodles.
options.C: Yes, dinner is the most important meal.
options.D: I'd like to apply for the part-time job there.

items.explanation: A 给出婉拒的理由——不饿，是间接拒绝的自然回应。B 谈拉面细节，回避邀请。C 错误意见。D 跳到打工话题。

items.situation: Printer in use
items.speaker: Are you almost done with the printer? I need to print my paper before class.

options.A: Just one more page — go ahead and queue your file.
options.B: The printer was replaced last semester.
options.C: Thanks, I appreciate the help.
options.D: I bought new ink cartridges last weekend.

items.explanation: A 直接解决问题——还有一页，可以排队。B 描述打印机历史，回避请求。C 答非所问。D 与情境无关。

items.situation: Inviting to study group
items.speaker: Would you want to join our biology study group on Wednesday evening?

options.A: Sure, what time should I show up?
options.B: Biology is one of the oldest sciences.
options.C: I left my notebook in the lab again.
options.D: Wednesday is the middle of the week.

items.explanation: A 接受邀请并询问时间。B 给出无关事实。C 谈遗失笔记本，偏题。D 关于星期的无意义评论。

items.situation: Need help in a stressful moment
items.speaker: I'm at my wits' end trying to make this registration form work.

options.A: Let me take a look — I had the same trouble last week.
options.B: Wits are an interesting topic in psychology.
options.C: Yes, the forms are printed on green paper now.
options.D: I'll bring my own form next time.

items.explanation: A 理解习语意为快崩溃，主动伸出援手。B 玩弄 wits 这个词，偏题。C 谈表格颜色，与困境无关。D 关于未来打印，回避当下求助。

items.situation: Asking why someone is quiet
items.speaker: You've been awfully quiet at lunch lately. Is everything okay?

options.A: Honestly, I've been worried about my thesis deadline.
options.B: Lunch starts at noon every day this week.
options.C: The cafeteria added new vegetarian options.
options.D: Yes, the room is usually quiet during exams.

items.explanation: A 诚实分享焦虑原因，符合关心提问。B 谈午餐时间，回避情感。C 谈菜单，无关。D 利用 quiet 重复，错读语境。

items.situation: Reporting a maintenance issue
items.speaker: My dorm window has been stuck open since last Friday.

options.A: Submit a quick request through the maintenance portal — they usually respond within a day.
options.B: Last Friday I went to the football game.
options.C: Dorms get especially warm in the spring.
options.D: Yes, windows are made of double-paned glass these days.

items.explanation: A 给出实际解决方案。B 回到上周五但偏题。C 谈宿舍温度，回避问题。D 关于玻璃材质，与情境无关。

items.situation: Asking about a deadline
items.speaker: Have you submitted your lab report yet?

options.A: Wait — was that due before today?
options.B: Reports should always include a hypothesis.
options.C: Yes, labs are scheduled for Tuesdays.
options.D: I usually wear gloves during the experiments.

items.explanation: A 反问截止日期，自然推进对话。B 谈一般写作建议，偏题。C 谈课时，回避问题。D 谈实验装束，无关。

items.situation: checking if a service (post office, bank) is open
items.speaker: The post office should still be open this late.

options.A: Actually, they closed at four today.
options.B: The mail truck just drove away.
options.C: I always mail my packages on Mondays.
options.D: Did you know it's a federal holiday tomorrow?

items.explanation: 正确答案以 Actually 引导，含蓄地纠正说话人的假设，符合 marker_led_indirect 范式。B 用了相关词 mail 但不回答；C 偏离话题；D 答非所问，错问回应。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: Why do we have to include the calibration data this time?

options.A: I forgot to submit my own report.
options.B: Because the new sensors drift more than last semester's.
options.C: The lab is closed for the holiday.
options.D: Calibration sounds like a useful word.

items.explanation: Why 问题直接回答原因，B 用 Because 引出新传感器漂移，符合 direct_topical。A 转向自身经历；C 离题；D 抓 calibration 这词造无意义回答。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: Have you been hearing that drilling next door all week?

options.A: Yes, I always drill before assignments.
options.B: The next door restaurant opens at noon.
options.C: I'll talk to the building manager today.
options.D: Drills are sold at the hardware store.

items.explanation: 正确答案不直接回答听到与否，而是直接解决问题（context_shift）。A 利用 drill 多义；B 利用 next door 但无关；D 是词汇陷阱。

items.situation: student asking about course enrollment or prerequisites
items.speaker: What courses do I need before taking organic chemistry?

options.A: Organic chemistry meets twice a week.
options.B: I bought my textbooks online.
options.C: Yes, the professor is really nice.
options.D: Actually, just general chemistry one and two.

items.explanation: 正确答案以 Actually 引导，直接说明前置课程。A 描述课程信息但答非所问；B 偏离；C 答错问题类型。

items.situation: friend inviting another to a study group or social event
items.speaker: Are you free Friday night for game night at our place?

options.A: Actually, I'm covering a friend's shift then.
options.B: Board games are quite popular here.
options.C: Yes, I love your place very much.
options.D: Did you watch the championship game?

items.explanation: 正确答案以 Actually 引导，含蓄拒绝并给出理由，符合 marker_led_indirect。B 抓 game；C 抓 place 但答错；D 抓 game 又答非所问。

items.situation: asking a store clerk about a return or exchange
items.speaker: I'd like to return this jacket, please.

options.A: Jackets are on the second floor.
options.B: Sure, do you have the receipt?
options.C: The store closes at nine tonight.
options.D: I prefer wool jackets in the winter.

items.explanation: 正确答案用反问推动对话（counter_question），询问凭据。A 给出与退货无关的位置；C 离题；D 表达个人偏好答非所问。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's situation
items.speaker: Where are you finding the time for all those clubs?

options.A: Our club meets in the basement Wednesdays.
options.B: The library closes at midnight on weeknights.
options.C: Honestly, I dropped two of my electives last week.
options.D: Time really does fly when finals approach.

items.explanation: 正确答案不直接回答地点，而是解释如何腾出时间（context_shift）。A、B 词汇陷阱；D 是套话答非所问。

items.situation: calling to check business hours
items.speaker: How late are you open on Sunday evenings?

options.A: Late nights are tough on weekends.
options.B: Sundays are usually slow around here.
options.C: We opened a second location last month.
options.D: Which Sunday are you planning to come?

items.explanation: 正确答案用反问推进对话，确认具体日期，符合 counter_question。A、B 给出主观或宽泛信息；C 离题。

items.situation: student seeking help at the writing center
items.speaker: Why does my thesis statement keep getting marked weak?

options.A: What's the main argument you're trying to make?
options.B: The writing center is open until six tonight.
options.C: I marked your last essay as well.
options.D: Where did you submit the paper?

items.explanation: 正确答案是A。说话人问论文主旨句为什么总被批薄弱，A用反问追问其核心论点，从而引导学生自己发现问题，是counter_question，推进对话。B选项是semantic_association_trap，由'writing center'联想到开放时间，但没有回应问题。C选项是polysemy_trap，把'marked'（批改）误用为另一处批改经历，答非所问。D选项是wrong_question_type，回答的是'在哪里提交'，而问的是'为什么'。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: Do we need to graph the results in the lab report?

options.A: The lab is on the second floor of the science hall.
options.B: I'll post a sample report on the course site tonight.
options.C: Yes, I graphed mine over the weekend.
options.D: When is the report actually due?

items.explanation: 正确答案是B。说话人不确定报告是否要画图，B没有直接回答是否，而是承诺发布一份样例报告，直接解决了'不知道格式要求'这个根本困惑，是context_shift。A选项是semantic_association_trap，由'lab'联想到实验室位置，但无关。C选项是bad pattern的反例规避后的off_topic，谈自己周末画图的经历，没有告诉对方要不要画。D选项是wrong_question_type，回答的是'什么时候交'，而问的是'要不要画图'。

items.situation: friend inviting another to a study group or social event
items.speaker: Are you coming to our study session at the cafe?

options.A: The cafe makes a really good latte.
options.B: I studied there all last semester.
options.C: What time are you all meeting up?
options.D: No, I haven't finished the reading.

items.explanation: 正确答案是C。说话人邀请去咖啡馆学习，C用反问询问集合时间，是counter_question，推进对话并表达愿意参加。A选项是semantic_association_trap，由'cafe'联想到拿铁，但无关。B选项是off_topic，谈自己上学期在那学习的经历，没有回应邀请。D选项是off_topic干扰，'No'但理由（没读完）与是否参加学习小组并不直接相关，未能推进安排。

items.situation: friend sharing news or personal update
items.speaker: I finally got into the architecture program.

options.A: The building downtown is brand new.
options.B: I got lost on the way over here.
options.C: Did you lock the door behind you?
options.D: That's fantastic, congratulations!

items.explanation: 正确答案是D。说话人分享被建筑专业录取的好消息，D用'That's fantastic, congratulations!'这一自然的祝贺习语回应，是idiomatic。A选项是semantic_association_trap，由'architecture'联想到楼房，但无关。B选项是polysemy/word_trap，重复了'got'但意思变成'迷路'，答非所问。C选项是off_topic，问是否锁门，与喜讯毫无关系。

items.situation: roommate discussing weekend plans
items.speaker: What should we do this weekend for a change?

options.A: How about that hiking trail you mentioned?
options.B: The weekend went by really fast last time.
options.C: I changed the bedsheets this morning.
options.D: Where did you put my charger?

items.explanation: 正确答案是A。室友问周末换个花样做什么，A用反问提出去对方提过的徒步小径，是counter_question，推进计划。B选项是semantic_association_trap，由'weekend'联想到上次周末过得快，但无关。C选项是polysemy_trap，把'change'（换换花样）误用为'换床单'，答非所问。D选项是off_topic，问充电器在哪，与周末计划无关。

items.situation: student asking about gym or sports facility hours
items.speaker: How early does the campus gym open on weekdays?

options.A: The gym just added a new climbing wall.
options.B: Actually, the hours are posted right by the door.
options.C: Yes, you can sign up for classes there.
options.D: Where do I find a locker for my stuff?

items.explanation: 正确答案是B。说话人问健身房工作日几点开门，B以话语标记'Actually'开头，间接指向门口贴着的时间表，是marker_led_indirect。A选项是semantic_association_trap，由'gym'联想到攀岩墙，但没回答时间。C选项是wrong_question_type，用'Yes'回答了一个是非问题，而问的是'多早'。D选项是off_topic，问储物柜位置，与开门时间无关。

items.situation: asking a store clerk about a return or exchange
items.speaker: I'd like to exchange these boots for a larger size.

options.A: These boots are very popular this season.
options.B: I bought a similar pair last winter.
options.C: Sure, let me check what we have in stock.
options.D: Where is the nearest shoe store?

items.explanation: 正确答案是C。顾客想把靴子换大一号，C直接答应并去查库存，是direct_topical。A选项是semantic_association_trap，由'boots'联想到流行款式，但没有处理换货请求。B选项是off_topic，店员谈自己买过类似的，与顾客请求无关。D选项是wrong_question_type，反问最近的鞋店在哪，答非所问。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: Where should I pick you up tomorrow morning?

options.A: The car needs an oil change soon.
options.B: I picked up some groceries earlier.
options.C: What time are we leaving anyway?
options.D: Maybe just outside my apartment building.

items.explanation: 正确答案是D。朋友问明早在哪接你，D以话语标记'Maybe'开头，间接给出地点（公寓楼外），是marker_led_indirect。A选项是semantic_association_trap，由'pick up'相关的'car'联想到换机油，但无关。B选项是polysemy_trap，把'pick up'（接人）误用为'买了些杂货'，答非所问。C选项是wrong_question_type，反问几点出发，而问的是'在哪里'。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: Why are you driving instead of taking the train?

options.A: Well, my car's actually cheaper for the three of us.
options.B: The train station has a new coffee stand.
options.C: Yes, I parked it right out front.
options.D: She drives to work every single morning.

items.explanation: 正确答案是A。说话人问为什么选择开车而不坐火车，A以话语标记'Well'开头，间接说明开车对三个人来说更便宜，给出了理由，是marker_led_indirect。B是semantic_association_trap，由'train'联想到火车站的咖啡摊，但没回答原因。C是wrong_question_type，回答的是'是否/停在哪'的是非或地点问题，而问的是'为什么'。D是off_topic，谈另一个人每天开车上班，与本对话无关。

items.situation: student checking lost-and-found at front desk
items.speaker: Did anyone turn in a gray umbrella today?

options.A: The weather app says it'll rain again tomorrow.
options.B: Let me take a look in the back for you.
options.C: Yes, I turned in my paper this morning.
options.D: The desk closes at five on Fridays.

items.explanation: 正确答案是B。说话人问今天有没有人交来一把灰色雨伞，B用自然的口语套话'Let me take a look'表示马上去后面帮忙查看，推进了对话并解决需求，是idiomatic。A是semantic_association_trap，由'umbrella'联想到天气下雨，但没去查。C是polysemy_trap，把'turn in'（上交失物）误用为'交论文'，答非所问。D是off_topic，谈前台周五关门时间，与是否有伞无关。

items.situation: student asking about a campus event or concert
items.speaker: Are tickets for the jazz concert still available?

options.A: The band played really well last spring.
options.B: No, I haven't listened to jazz before.
options.C: Actually, they sold out about an hour ago.
options.D: The hall is across from the library.

items.explanation: 正确答案是C。说话人问爵士音乐会的票是否还有，C以话语标记'Actually'开头，间接说明大约一小时前已售罄，自然回应了是否还有票，是marker_led_indirect。A是semantic_association_trap，由'concert'联想到乐队上次的演出，但没回答票务。B是wrong_question_type，回答的是'你听没听过爵士'，而问的是'有没有票'。D是off_topic，谈演出厅位置，与票是否还有无关。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: I'm not sure how long the report should be.

options.A: The lab closes at nine on weeknights.
options.B: Yes, I finished writing mine already.
options.C: Reports are due at the end of the term.
options.D: Well, four to five pages is usually plenty.

items.explanation: 正确答案是D。说话人不确定报告该写多长，D以话语标记'Well'开头，间接给出四到五页通常就够了，直接回应了篇幅困惑，是marker_led_indirect。A是semantic_association_trap，由'report/lab'联想到实验室关门时间，但无关。B是off_topic，谈自己已写完，没告诉对方该多长。C是wrong_question_type，回答的是'什么时候交'，而问的是'写多长'。

items.situation: student asking about exam format or study materials
items.speaker: Where can I find practice problems for the final?

options.A: I'll email everyone a full review packet tonight.
options.B: The final is scheduled for next Thursday.
options.C: Yes, practice really does help a lot.
options.D: The exam room is in the math building.

items.explanation: 正确答案是A。说话人问去哪里找期末练习题，A没有直接给地点，而是承诺今晚把完整复习资料发邮件给大家，直接解决了'找不到练习题'这个根本需求，是context_shift。B是semantic_association_trap，由'final'联想到考试时间，但没回答去哪找。C是wrong_question_type，回答的是'练习是否有用'的是非问题，而问的是'在哪里'。D是off_topic，谈考场位置，与练习题来源无关。

items.situation: checking if a service (post office, bank) is open
items.speaker: What time does the bank close on Saturdays?

options.A: The bank just remodeled its lobby last month.
options.B: Actually, it shuts at noon on weekends.
options.C: Yes, you can open an account there.
options.D: It's right beside the grocery store.

items.explanation: 正确答案是B。说话人问银行周六几点关门，B以话语标记'Actually'开头，间接说明周末中午就关，直接回应了关门时间，是marker_led_indirect。A是semantic_association_trap，由'bank'联想到上月装修大堂，但没回答时间。C是wrong_question_type，回答的是'能否开户'的是非问题，而问的是'几点关门'。D是off_topic，谈银行旁边是杂货店，回答的是位置而非时间。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: I missed today's lecture and need the notes.

options.A: The lecture hall was packed this morning.
options.B: Yes, I take notes in every class.
options.C: Sure, I'll send you a photo tonight.
options.D: The professor speaks pretty fast.

items.explanation: 正确答案是C。说话人错过了今天的课需要笔记，C以'Sure'应允并说今晚发照片过去，间接而自然地满足了借笔记的请求，是marker_led_indirect。A是semantic_association_trap，由'lecture'联想到课堂很满，但没回应请求。B是off_topic，谈自己每节课都记笔记，没说能否借出。D是off_topic，谈教授语速快，与借笔记无关。

items.situation: friend inviting another to a study group or social event
items.speaker: How about joining our study group tonight?

options.A: The group meets in the science building.
options.B: Yes, I studied biology last year.
options.C: We have about six members right now.
options.D: Count me in, I could use the help.

items.explanation: 正确答案是D。说话人邀请对方今晚加入学习小组，D用自然口语习语'Count me in'表示乐意参加，并补充自己正需要帮助，是idiomatic，欣然接受邀请。A是semantic_association_trap，由'study group'联想到聚会地点，但没回应邀请。B是polysemy_trap/off_topic，把话题岔到去年学生物，答非所问。C是off_topic，谈小组现有成员数，没回应是否参加。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: Why don't we just carpool to the airport together?

options.A: Sounds like a plan to me.
options.B: The airport has a new parking garage.
options.C: I missed my flight last spring.
options.D: Every twenty minutes, I think.

items.explanation: A 用习惯表达 'Sounds like a plan' 自然地答应提议，正确。B 借 'airport' 设词陷阱；C 离题谈过去误机；D 答成频率，答非所问。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Do you have this textbook available as an ebook?

options.A: The library closes at nine tonight.
options.B: Actually, let me check the catalog for you.
options.C: I returned my library books last week.
options.D: The printer is out of paper again.

items.explanation: B 以 'Actually' 开头，间接地提出去查目录，正确回应。A 借 'library' 答营业时间，词陷阱；C、D 都离题谈还书和打印机。

items.situation: student checking with a TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: How long should the lab report actually be?

options.A: Reports are usually graded within a week.
options.B: The lab is on the third floor.
options.C: I'll email you the full assignment sheet.
options.D: Yes, you should definitely include graphs.

items.explanation: C 不直接答字数，而是发完整作业要求解决根本需求，符合 context_shift。A 答成批改时间，问非所答；B 借 'lab' 设词陷阱；D 对 how-long 问题答成 yes，答非所问。

items.situation: asking a store clerk about a return or exchange
items.speaker: These shoes are too small, unfortunately.

options.A: Shoes are all on sale this weekend.
options.B: I walk to work every single morning.
options.C: Yes, they do look brand new.
options.D: I can grab you the next size up.

items.explanation: D 不纠结字面，而是直接提出拿大一号解决问题，符合 context_shift。A 借 'shoes' 设词陷阱；B 离题谈走路上班；C 只附和却不回应需求。

items.situation: calling to check a shop's business hours
items.speaker: Where can I find your opening hours online?

options.A: Actually, they're listed on our homepage.
options.B: The store is right next to the bank.
options.C: We close at six on weekdays.
options.D: I love shopping there on weekends.

items.explanation: A 以 'Actually' 引出在主页能查到，正面回应了 where。B 答店铺位置，借词陷阱、答错方面；C 答成关门时间，问非所答；D 离题表达喜好。

items.situation: checking whether a service like the post office is still open
items.speaker: I really need to mail this package today.

options.A: The package looks a little damaged.
options.B: Do you want me to drive you there?
options.C: I sent a letter just last Tuesday.
options.D: Yes, stamps have gotten so expensive.

items.explanation: B 用反问主动提出开车送你，推进对话并解决需求，符合 counter_question。A 借 'package' 设词陷阱；C、D 离题谈寄信和邮票价格。

items.situation: a classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: What did I miss in today's lecture?

options.A: The lecture hall was freezing today.
options.B: I take all my notes on a laptop.
options.C: Want me to send you my notes?
options.D: Class starts at ten tomorrow morning.

items.explanation: C 用反问提出把笔记发给你，自然推进对话，符合 counter_question。A 借 'lecture' 设词陷阱；B 离题谈记笔记方式；D 答上课时间，答非所问。

items.situation: a student checking the lost-and-found at a front desk
items.speaker: Has anyone turned in a blue water bottle?

options.A: The water fountain is down the hall.
options.B: I lost my keys here just yesterday.
options.C: Yes, lost items really pile up fast.
options.D: Could you tell me where you left it?

items.explanation: D 用反问请对方说明丢失地点以便查找，推进对话，符合 counter_question。A 借 'water' 设词陷阱；B 离题谈丢钥匙；C 只感慨却不帮忙。

items.situation: student asking about exam format or study materials
items.speaker: What's the exam format going to be this term?

options.A: Your guess is as good as mine.
options.B: The exam room is on the second floor.
options.C: Yes, the term ends in December.
options.D: I formatted my essay last night.

items.explanation: 正确答案A是一个地道的口语习语，意思是"我也不清楚"，自然地回应了对方的疑问，符合idiomatic范式。B是语义联想陷阱，利用了"exam"联想到考场地点，却没有回答格式问题。C回答的是"什么时候"而非"什么格式"，属于答非所问，且"term"是关键词陷阱。D利用了"format"一词的双关（排版/格式），转向了无关的论文排版，属于多义词误读。

items.situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline
items.speaker: Why is our project due so early this time?

options.A: The project room is always crowded.
options.B: Because the professor's traveling next week.
options.C: I finished my part of the slides.
options.D: Yes, I think we should start soon.

items.explanation: 正确答案B直接回答了"为什么"提前——因为教授下周要出差，符合direct_topical范式。A是语义联想陷阱，由"project"联想到项目教室，却没解释原因。C陈述了一个无关的个人事实（自己完成了幻灯片），属于离题但语法正确的干扰项。D用"Yes"回答了一个"为什么"的问题，属于答非所问的问题类型错误。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Do you have the second edition of this textbook?

options.A: The textbook cover is bright red.
options.B: I edited that chapter myself.
options.C: Would the third edition work for you?
options.D: No, I haven't read it yet.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用一个反问来推进对话——询问对方第三版是否可以，巧妙地暗示第二版可能没有但有替代，符合counter_question范式。A是语义联想陷阱，由"textbook"联想到书的封面颜色，毫无帮助。B利用了"edition"和"edited"的词形相近，是关键词陷阱兼多义误读。D回答的是"你有没有读过"，而非"你这里有没有"，属于回答了错误的问题。

items.situation: student asking about a reading list for a course
items.speaker: I can't make heads or tails of this reading list.

options.A: Reading by the window is so relaxing.
options.B: The list was posted on Tuesday.
options.C: Heads up, the bookstore is closed.
options.D: Let's go through it together.

items.explanation: 说话人用习语"can't make heads or tails of"表示"完全看不懂"。正确答案D提议"一起来看看"，是对困惑最自然、最有帮助的回应，符合idiomatic情境下的助人范式。A是语义联想陷阱，由"reading"联想到阅读这件事本身，离题。B回答的是清单何时发布，属于答非所问。C用了"heads"一词制造关键词陷阱，转向无关的书店信息。

items.situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed
items.speaker: Are you doing okay? You seem really tense.

options.A: Honestly, I've just got a lot on my plate.
options.B: The tent is set up by the lake.
options.C: Yes, ten people are coming tonight.
options.D: I'm okay with the new schedule.

items.explanation: 正确答案A以话语标记"Honestly"开头，间接承认自己确实压力大（"a lot on my plate"是"事情很多"的习语），符合marker_led_indirect范式。B利用"tense"与"tent"读音相近制造关键词陷阱，转向无关的帐篷。C利用"tense"与"ten"的谐音，回答了无关的人数。D表面用了"okay"，但回应的是对新日程的态度，属于错误语境。

items.situation: student asking about course enrollment or prerequisites
items.speaker: Where do I sign up for the prerequisite course?

options.A: The course covers a lot of material.
options.B: Actually, you can do it all online now.
options.C: Yes, that's a required class.
options.D: I signed my lease last weekend.

items.explanation: 正确答案B以话语标记"Actually"开头，间接告知报名地点——现在都在网上办理，巧妙地回应了"在哪报名"，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A是语义联想陷阱，由"course"联想到课程内容，没有回答地点。C用"Yes"回答了"在哪里"的问题，属于答非所问。D利用"sign up"与"signed"词形相近，转向无关的租房合同，是关键词陷阱兼多义误读。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: I'm not sure if we need to include raw data.

options.A: The lab smelled like chemicals today.
options.B: Raw vegetables are healthier, they say.
options.C: Well, the rubric says to attach it.
options.D: Did you finish the experiment already?

items.explanation: 正确答案C以话语标记"Well"开头，间接给出依据——评分标准要求附上原始数据，自然回应了对方的不确定，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A是语义联想陷阱，由"lab"联想到实验室的气味，离题。B利用"raw"一词的双关（原始数据/生的蔬菜），转向无关的饮食话题，属于多义误读。D反问对方是否做完实验，回避了数据问题，属于离题/错误问题。

items.situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: How do I report the broken heater in my room?

options.A: The room gets really cold at night.
options.B: Yes, the heater is quite old.
options.C: I reported my lost keycard yesterday.
options.D: Have you tried the maintenance app?

items.explanation: 正确答案D用反问推进对话——建议对方试试维修App，间接告诉了"怎么报修"，符合counter_question范式。A是语义联想陷阱，由"heater"联想到房间寒冷，没有回答方法。B用"Yes"回答了一个"怎么做"的问题，属于答非所问。C利用"report"一词，转向无关的丢失门卡事件，是关键词陷阱兼离题。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: Why aren't you driving to the concert tonight?

options.A: Well, my car's still at the shop.
options.B: The concert hall seats two thousand.
options.C: Yes, the tickets sold out fast.
options.D: I drove past there this morning.

items.explanation: 正确答案A以话语标记"Well"开头，间接说明不开车的原因——车还在修理店，符合marker_led_indirect范式。B是语义联想陷阱，由"concert"联想到音乐厅座位数，没有回答原因。C用"Yes"回答了一个"为什么"的问题，属于答非所问的问题类型错误。D利用"driving"与"drove"词形相近，转向无关的早晨开车经过，是关键词陷阱兼离题。

items.situation: making or rescheduling an appointment
items.speaker: I'd like to move my dentist appointment to Friday.

options.A: The dentist's office has new chairs.
options.B: Sure, I can fit you in at two.
options.C: I moved into my new place last month.
options.D: Yes, brushing twice a day really helps.

items.explanation: 正确答案B直接答应了改期请求，并给出周五两点的具体时间，符合direct_topical范式。A是语义联想陷阱，由"dentist"联想到诊所的新椅子，没有处理改期。C利用"move"一词的双关（改期/搬家），转向无关的搬家事件，属于多义误读。D离题谈刷牙习惯，是语法正确但毫无关联的句子。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: Where did you put your notes from Monday's class?

options.A: The classroom was freezing on Monday.
options.B: I noted the deadline in my planner.
options.C: They're right at your fingertips.
options.D: Yes, I attended every lecture.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用习语"right at your fingertips"（近在手边、随时可取）地道地回应了对方找笔记的需求，符合idiomatic范式。A是语义联想陷阱，由"class"联想到教室很冷，离题。B利用"notes"与"noted"词形相近，转向无关的记录截止日期，是关键词陷阱兼多义误读。D用"Yes"回答了一个"在哪里"的问题，属于答非所问的问题类型错误。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: How should we format the references section?

options.A: The lab ran out of beakers today.
options.B: Yes, references are very important.
options.C: I referred my roommate to that course.
options.D: Just follow the APA style guide.

items.explanation: 正确答案D以话语标记"Just"开头，简洁地给出格式依据——按APA风格指南，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A是语义联想陷阱，由"lab"联想到实验室缺烧杯，离题。B用"Yes"加上无关评价回应了一个"怎么做"的问题，属于答非所问。C利用"references"与"referred"词形相近，转向无关的推荐室友选课，是关键词陷阱兼多义误读。

items.situation: checking if a service (post office, bank) is open
items.speaker: The post office probably isn't open this late.

options.A: There's a drop box right outside.
options.B: The post office repainted its sign.
options.C: I posted those photos online already.
options.D: Yes, the line was really long today.

items.explanation: 正确答案A不纠结于营业时间，而是解决了对方的潜在需求——门口就有投递箱可以寄信，符合context_shift范式。B是语义联想陷阱，由"post office"联想到重新粉刷招牌，毫无帮助。C利用"post"一词的双关（邮局/发帖），转向无关的网上发照片，属于多义误读。D虽用"Yes"，却离题谈今天排队很长，属于离题但语法正确的干扰项。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's situation
items.speaker: Have you been sleeping at all this week?

options.A: The sheets are in the dryer now.
options.B: Why, do I look that tired?
options.C: Yes, I sleep on the left side.
options.D: I weakened the coffee this morning.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用一个反问"我看起来有那么累吗？"推进了对话，自然回应了对方的关心，符合counter_question范式。A是语义联想陷阱，由"sleeping"联想到床单晾在烘干机里，离题。C用"Yes"加无关细节回答，回应的是睡哪一侧而非是否睡了，属于答非所问。D利用"week"与"weakened"的谐音，转向无关的早晨冲咖啡，是关键词陷阱兼离题。

items.situation: two friends deciding where to get lunch
items.speaker: Do you want to try the new noodle place today?

options.A: The kitchen smelled amazing earlier.
options.B: I tried out for the team last fall.
options.C: How about that taco truck instead?
options.D: Yes, I finished my noodles already.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用反问提出了另一个选择——那辆塔可车，推进了关于去哪吃的对话，符合counter_question范式。A是语义联想陷阱，由"noodle place"联想到厨房的香味，没有作出决定。B利用"try"与"tried out"词形相近，转向无关的参加球队选拔，是关键词陷阱兼多义误读。D虽用"Yes"，却谈自己已经吃完了面，属于错误语境/离题。

items.situation: friend sharing news or personal update
items.speaker: What made you decide to move across the country?

options.A: The country roads are so scenic.
options.B: Yes, it's a very big decision.
options.C: I moved the couch by myself.
options.D: Well, I finally landed my dream job.

items.explanation: 正确答案D以话语标记"Well"开头，间接给出搬家的原因——终于得到了梦寐以求的工作，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A是语义联想陷阱，由"country"联想到乡间小路风景，没有回答原因。B用"Yes"加无关评价回应了一个"是什么原因"的问题，属于答非所问。C利用"move"一词的双关（搬迁/搬动），转向无关的搬沙发，属于多义误读。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's overwhelming schedule
items.speaker: You've really been burning the candle at both ends.

options.A: Yeah, I'm running on empty lately.
options.B: The candles are on the kitchen shelf.
options.C: I lit both of them last night.
options.D: She left around the same time.

items.explanation: 正确答案 A 用习语 "running on empty" 回应对方关于自己过度劳累的关心，自然承认精疲力竭，符合 idiomatic 范式。B 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "candle" 这个词去谈论实物蜡烛，完全误读了习语含义。C 也是语义陷阱，把 "both ends" 字面理解成点了两支蜡烛。D 是离题但语法正确的句子，谈论另一个人在另一场景，与对话无关。

items.situation: student asking a librarian whether a journal can be accessed
items.speaker: Do you have access to last year's conference papers?

options.A: The conference was held downtown.
options.B: Let me check that for you.
options.C: I attended a few of those talks.
options.D: Papers are due at the front desk.

items.explanation: 正确答案 B 用自然的会话套语 "Let me check that for you" 回应学生的请求，是图书管理员典型的助人回答，符合 idiomatic 范式。A 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "conference" 去谈会议地点，没回答能否获取。C 也是语义陷阱，谈自己参加过相关讲座，听上去相关却没回答问题。D 是误用关键词 "papers"，把它理解成要上交的作业，答非所问。

items.situation: student asking why the lab printer keeps failing
items.speaker: Why won't this printer connect to my laptop?

options.A: The printer is brand new this term.
options.B: Yes, you can scan documents here.
options.C: Actually, you have to install the driver first.
options.D: I printed my essay yesterday morning.

items.explanation: 正确答案 C 以话语标记 "Actually" 开头，间接给出连接失败的原因和解决办法，符合 marker_led_indirect 范式。A 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "printer" 去描述打印机很新，没解决连接问题。B 是答错问题类型，学生问 why，却用 yes 回答另一个 can 类问题。D 是离题但语法正确的句子，谈自己昨天打印过，与当前连接问题无关。

items.situation: student mentioning an upcoming campus concert
items.speaker: There's a free jazz concert in the quad tonight.

options.A: The quad was just repaved last week.
options.B: Jazz started in New Orleans, you know.
options.C: Tickets are usually pretty expensive.
options.D: Want to grab dinner and head over?

items.explanation: 正确答案 D 用反问 "Want to grab dinner and head over?" 推进对话，提出一起去的计划，符合 counter_question 范式。A 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "quad" 去谈广场重铺路面，与音乐会无关。B 也是语义陷阱，抓住 "jazz" 去讲爵士乐历史，离题。C 谈门票很贵，但对方明说是 free 免费的，属于误读语境的离题回答。

items.situation: student asking whether the late shuttle still runs
items.speaker: Does the campus shuttle still run after nine?

options.A: Why not just walk with me instead?
options.B: The shuttle is painted bright blue.
options.C: I lost my bus pass somewhere yesterday.
options.D: Yes, the driver seemed friendly enough.

items.explanation: 正确答案 A 用反问 "Why not just walk with me instead?" 推进对话，提出替代方案，符合 counter_question 范式。B 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "shuttle" 去描述它的颜色，没回答时间问题。C 是离题但语法正确的句子，谈丢了公交卡，与运营时间无关。D 是误读语境，虽然以 yes 开头，但谈的是司机态度而非班车是否还运行，答非所问。

items.situation: student seeking help with an essay at the writing center
items.speaker: How can I make my thesis statement stronger?

options.A: The writing center closes at six today.
options.B: Well, try making your claim more specific.
options.C: Yes, statements should be grammatically correct.
options.D: I wrote three essays last semester.

items.explanation: 正确答案 B 以话语标记 "Well" 开头，间接给出让论点更有力的具体建议，符合 marker_led_indirect 范式。A 是离题但语法正确的句子，谈写作中心关门时间，没回答如何改进。C 是答错问题类型，学生问 how，却用 yes 回答，且只泛谈语法正确。D 是离题句子，谈自己上学期写过几篇文章，与当前论点修改无关。

items.situation: two friends deciding where to get lunch
items.speaker: Where should we grab lunch before class?

options.A: I had a huge breakfast this morning.
options.B: Class starts at noon on Tuesdays.
options.C: Maybe that new cafe by the gym.
options.D: Lunch boxes are sold near the entrance.

items.explanation: 正确答案 C 以话语标记 "Maybe" 开头，间接给出吃饭地点的建议，符合 marker_led_indirect 范式。A 是离题但语法正确的句子，谈自己早餐吃得多，没回答去哪吃午饭。B 是答错问题类型，对方问 where，却回答 when 上课时间。D 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "lunch" 去谈盒饭售卖处，没真正回答去哪儿吃。

items.situation: student checking with the TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: What should we include in the lab report?

options.A: The lab is on the second floor.
options.B: Yes, reports are graded out of fifty.
options.C: I finished my report a week ago.
options.D: Just add your methods and results.

items.explanation: 正确答案 D 以话语标记 "Just" 开头，简洁间接地说明报告该包含的内容，符合 marker_led_indirect 范式。A 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "lab" 去谈实验室楼层位置，没回答内容要求。B 是答错问题类型，学生问 what，却用 yes 回答，且只谈评分。C 是离题但语法正确的句子，谈自己一周前写完了报告，与该写什么无关。

items.situation: friend sharing news or personal update
items.speaker: I finally got the job at the museum.

options.A: Well, that's the news I was hoping for.
options.B: The museum has a new dinosaur exhibit.
options.C: I lost my job there last spring.
options.D: Where did you leave your application?

items.explanation: 正确答案A以话语标记词"Well"开头，间接地表达了为朋友感到高兴和欣慰，是对好消息的自然回应。B是语义联想陷阱，抓住"museum"扯到展览，没有回应这个好消息。C跑题，谈的是说话人自己丢工作的过去事件，情感和情境都不匹配。D答非所问，对方分享的是已经得到工作的结果，却问申请放在哪里，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: How can I get your notes from today's lecture?

options.A: The lecture hall was completely full today.
options.B: Sure, I'll email them to you tonight.
options.C: I take notes on my laptop now.
options.D: When does the next lecture start?

items.explanation: 正确答案B以"Sure"开头，间接答应并说明具体方式（晚上发邮件），自然推进对话。A是语义联想陷阱，抓住"lecture"描述讲堂很满，没有回应借笔记的请求。C跑题，只说自己用笔记本电脑记笔记，并没有回答怎么把笔记给对方。D答非所问，对方问怎么拿到笔记，却回答下节课什么时候开始，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: student asking about campus shuttle or parking
items.speaker: What's the fastest way to reach the north lot?

options.A: The lot was just repaved over the weekend.
options.B: Yes, parking permits are required there.
options.C: Your best bet is the shuttle out front.
options.D: I parked there once last semester.

items.explanation: 正确答案C用了习语"Your best bet is..."（你最好的选择是），自然地给出最快的方式——坐门口的班车。A是语义联想陷阱，抓住"lot"说停车场刚重铺，没回答怎么去。B答非所问，对方问"怎么去"却用"Yes"回答许可证问题，属于错误问题类型。D跑题，只说自己上学期在那停过车，没有提供到达方式。

items.situation: student seeking help at the writing center
items.speaker: Can someone here look over my essay draft?

options.A: I wrote three essays last term.
options.B: The center moved to the second floor.
options.C: Are you taking the writing seminar?
options.D: Yes, a tutor can read it with you now.

items.explanation: 正确答案D直接回答了问题——是的，现在有辅导员可以陪你看草稿。A跑题，只谈自己上学期写了三篇文章，与当下求助无关。B是语义联想陷阱，抓住"center"说写作中心搬到二楼，没回答能不能帮看草稿。C答非所问，对方问能否帮忙看，却反问是否在上写作研讨课，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: student asking about a campus event or concert
items.speaker: The spring concert is happening this Friday.

options.A: Actually, I already bought us two tickets.
options.B: The concert hall seats about a thousand.
options.C: I missed last year's spring festival.
options.D: Where do you usually buy your groceries?

items.explanation: 正确答案A以话语标记词"Actually"开头，间接回应说自己已经买好两张票，自然推进对话并暗示一起去。B是语义联想陷阱，抓住"concert"说音乐厅能坐多少人，没回应这个消息。C跑题，谈的是自己错过去年春季活动的过去事件。D完全跑题，问对方平时在哪买杂货，与音乐会毫无关系。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: Do you have the second edition of this textbook?

options.A: I read that textbook over the summer.
options.B: Would the third edition work for you instead?
options.C: The reading room closes at eight tonight.
options.D: Yes, I know several students using it.

items.explanation: 正确答案B用反问推进对话，提出第三版是否也可以，是图书馆员处理"有没有某版本"的自然方式。A跑题，只说自己暑假读过这本书，没回答馆藏问题。C是语义联想陷阱，抓住图书馆情境说阅览室八点关门，与版本无关。D是审计中常见的歧义陷阱类型——用"know"的"认识"义（认识几个用书的学生），并没有真正回答是否有这一版，属于多义/误读陷阱。

items.situation: calling to check business hours
items.speaker: Where can I find your weekend opening hours?

options.A: The weekend crowds are usually pretty heavy.
options.B: Yes, we open quite early on Saturdays.
options.C: Don't worry, we're open all weekend long.
options.D: The store moved to a new location downtown.

items.explanation: 正确答案C是情境转移——不纠结于"在哪查"，而是直接解决背后的真实需求（用"Don't worry"安抚并告知周末全天营业）。A是语义联想陷阱，抓住"weekend"说周末人很多，没解决需求。B答非所问，对方问"在哪查营业时间"，却用"Yes"回答，属于错误问题类型。D跑题，说店搬到市中心新址，与营业时间无关。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: Why is there banging coming from upstairs?

options.A: The upstairs unit has a lovely balcony.
options.B: Banging on doors is rude, I agree.
options.C: When did you move into this building?
options.D: I'll go ask them to keep it down.

items.explanation: 正确答案D是情境转移——不解释为什么有声音，而是直接解决背后的困扰（我去让他们小声点）。A是语义联想陷阱，抓住"upstairs"夸楼上阳台漂亮，无视困扰。B误读了意图，把"banging"理解成敲门并空泛附和，没有解决问题。C答非所问，对方问声音原因，却反问你什么时候搬进来的，属于错误问题类型。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: My car's in the shop until Thursday.

options.A: Want me to drive you in the meantime?
options.B: The shop downtown has great prices.
options.C: I just got my oil changed too.
options.D: Yes, the weather's been pretty bad.

items.explanation: 说话人是在暗示自己这几天没车、需要帮助。A 用反问提出主动载对方，推进了对话并解决了潜在需求，是最恰当的回应。B 抓住‘shop（修车店）’这个词转而谈论店家价格，属于语义联想陷阱，没有回应需求。C 重复‘car/oil’相关话题，谈自己换机油，属于语义联想陷阱且答非所问。D 用‘yes’回应一个并非是非问句的陈述，且转到天气，属于答错问题类型，与缺车无关。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: I'm not sure how detailed the methods section should be.

options.A: The lab is open until six tonight.
options.B: Better safe than sorry—include everything.
options.C: I bought a new lab notebook yesterday.
options.D: Methods can be tricky to memorize.

items.explanation: 说话人在不确定方法部分要写多详细，B 用习惯用语‘Better safe than sorry’给出建议——宁可写全，自然又贴切地给出指导。A 抓住‘lab’联想到实验室开放时间，属于语义联想陷阱，没回应内容。C 谈自己买了实验记录本，重复‘lab’相关词，属于语义联想陷阱。D 把‘methods’误读为需要‘背诵’的东西，属于一词多义/误解陷阱，偏离了‘要写多详细’这个问题。

items.situation: roommate discussing weekend plans
items.speaker: How do you feel about a road trip this weekend?

options.A: The road near campus is always busy.
options.B: I felt sick after the last trip.
options.C: Count me in—I could use a break.
options.D: Weekends are only two days long.

items.explanation: 说话人在邀请对方周末自驾游，C 用习惯说法‘Count me in’热情答应并补充理由，是自然又恰当的回应。A 抓住‘road’联想到校园附近的路很堵，属于语义联想陷阱。B 把‘feel’误解为身体感受，谈上次旅行后生病，属于一词多义/误解陷阱，没回应这次邀请。D 重复‘weekend’却只是陈述周末有两天，属于答非所问的离题回答。

items.situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline
items.speaker: Did the professor push our deadline back?

options.A: The professor's lecture ran long today.
options.B: Deadlines are stressful for everyone.
options.C: Yes, I really enjoy group projects.
options.D: Why don't we just ask her after class?

items.explanation: 说话人不确定截止日期是否延后，D 用反问提议课后直接去问教授，推进了对话并指向解决办法，是最恰当的回应。A 抓住‘professor’联想到今天讲课拖堂，属于语义联想陷阱。B 抓住‘deadline’泛泛感慨压力大，属于语义联想陷阱，没给信息。C 用‘yes’但答非所问，回答的是‘你喜不喜欢小组项目’这类完全不同的问题，属于答错问题类型。

items.situation: student checking lost-and-found at front desk
items.speaker: Why didn't anyone turn in my umbrella?

options.A: Well, things often show up a day later.
options.B: The umbrella shop is across the street.
options.C: It rained pretty hard this morning.
options.D: I turned in my keys last week.

items.explanation: 说话人在抱怨没人上交他的雨伞，A 以话语标记‘Well’开头，间接安抚说东西常常晚一天才出现，是自然、得体的回应。B 抓住‘umbrella’联想到对面的伞店，属于语义联想陷阱。C 抓住‘umbrella/rain’联想到早上下大雨，属于语义联想陷阱，没回应问题。D 重复‘turn in’却谈自己上周交了钥匙，属于答非所问的离题回答。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: Where can I find the reading you mentioned in class?

options.A: The reading room is on the second floor.
options.B: I'll just email it to you right now.
options.C: That class was unusually crowded today.
options.D: Yes, the reading was quite long.

items.explanation: 说话人问哪里能找到课上提到的阅读材料，B 不直接回答地点，而是直接解决其根本需求——立刻把材料发邮件给他，属于情境转移，是最佳回应。A 把‘reading’误读为‘阅读室’并谈其楼层，属于一词多义/语义联想陷阱。C 抓住‘class’联想到今天人很多，属于离题。D 用‘yes’但答非所问，回应的是‘阅读是不是很长’这类不同问题，属于答错问题类型。

items.situation: student discussing a presentation topic with partner
items.speaker: What topic should we pick for our presentation?

options.A: The presentation hall seats two hundred.
options.B: I picked up coffee for us earlier.
options.C: Let's do renewable energy—it's timely.
options.D: Yes, presentations make me nervous.

items.explanation: 说话人在问演讲该选什么主题，C 直接给出具体建议‘可再生能源’并说明理由，是直接而恰当的回答。A 抓住‘presentation’联想到报告厅座位数，属于语义联想陷阱。B 把‘pick’误解为‘pick up（取咖啡）’，属于一词多义/误解陷阱，与选题无关。D 用‘yes’但答非所问，回应的是‘紧不紧张’，属于答错问题类型。

items.situation: student seeking help at the writing center
items.speaker: Can someone help me tighten up my conclusion?

options.A: The center closes early on Fridays.
options.B: Conclusions usually go at the end.
options.C: Yes, I tightened the screws yesterday.
options.D: Sure, I'd be happy to take a look.

items.explanation: 说话人在写作中心请人帮忙润色结尾，D 用习惯客气的表达‘Sure, I'd be happy to take a look’欣然答应帮忙，是自然得体的回应。A 抓住‘center’联想到周五早关门，属于语义联想陷阱。B 泛泛陈述结论位置，属于语义联想/离题，没提供帮助。C 把‘tighten’误读为‘拧紧螺丝’，属于一词多义/误解陷阱。

items.situation: responding to an invitation or suggestion
items.speaker: What can I bring that won't be a hassle?

options.A: Honestly, don't bring a thing — it's covered.
options.B: The party store downtown has great decorations.
options.C: I finally finished painting my bedroom.
options.D: It starts around seven in the evening.

items.explanation: 说话人问带什么东西来才不会太麻烦，暗示想帮忙分担。正确答案A没有纠结'带什么'这个字面问题，而是直接说什么都不用带、全都准备好了，从根本上打消了对方的顾虑，符合context_shift范式。B项借用'bring/party'的联想，谈起市中心派对用品店的装饰，属于语义联想陷阱，答非所问。C项完全离题，说自己刚粉刷完卧室。D项回答的是'几点开始'而非'带什么'，属于答错问题类型。

items.situation: making or rescheduling an appointment
items.speaker: Where's the office to reschedule my checkup?

options.A: My schedule's been packed all month.
options.B: You've come to the right place, actually.
options.C: Sometime early next week works best.
options.D: I still need to renew my parking permit.

items.explanation: 说话人问去哪个办公室改约体检时间。正确答案B使用习惯性表达'You've come to the right place'（你问对地方/人了），既自然又暗示对方现在就能在这里帮忙办理，符合idiomatic范式。A项借用'reschedule'联想到'schedule'，却谈自己这个月日程很满，属于语义联想陷阱。C项回答的是'什么时候改约'而非'去哪里'，答错了问题类型。D项完全离题，说自己还得去续办停车证。

items.situation: student asking about campus shuttle or parking
items.speaker: I can't ever figure out the shuttle schedule.

options.A: The shuttle got a fresh coat of paint.
options.B: I left my umbrella in the library again.
options.C: Actually, an app tracks it live now.
options.D: The new figures came out in the report.

items.explanation: 说话人抱怨自己老是搞不懂校车时刻表。正确答案C以话语标记'Actually'开头，间接给出解决办法——现在有个App能实时追踪校车，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A项借用'shuttle'一词，却谈校车重新喷了漆，属于语义联想陷阱。B项完全离题，说自己又把伞落在图书馆了。D项把'figure'（弄明白）误当成'figures'（数字/数据），谈报告里出了新数据，属于一词多义误解型干扰项。

items.situation: acknowledging someone's unavailability or schedule conflict
items.speaker: I won't be able to make it Friday night.

options.A: Friday's forecast looks pretty stormy.
options.B: I just repotted all my plants yesterday.
options.C: Great, I'll see you there then!
options.D: Oh, we'll catch you next time then.

items.explanation: 说话人告知自己周五晚上来不了。正确答案D以话语标记'Oh'开头，温暖地表示没关系、下次再聚，得体地回应了对方的爽约，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A项借用'Friday'一词，却谈周五天气预报有暴风雨，属于语义联想陷阱。B项完全离题，说自己昨天给植物换了盆。C项把对方的话误解成'会到场'，回复'太好了到时见'，与对方明说来不了正好相反，属于误解型干扰项。

items.situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: How do I report a heater that's broken?

options.A: Just give the front desk a shout.
options.B: The heater in the lounge is really loud.
options.C: I signed up for the cooking club today.
options.D: My lab report is due this Friday.

items.explanation: 说话人问宿舍暖气坏了该怎么报修。正确答案A使用口语习惯表达'give the front desk a shout'（跟前台说一声），自然地给出了报修的办法，符合idiomatic范式。B项借用'heater'一词，却谈休息室的暖气很吵，属于语义联想陷阱。C项完全离题，说自己今天报名了烹饪社团。D项把'report'（报修）误当成'lab report'（实验报告），属于一词多义误解型干扰项。

items.situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed
items.speaker: Why are you carrying all those boxes yourself?

options.A: Those boxes are made of recycled cardboard.
options.B: Here, let me grab a couple.
options.C: I watched a great documentary last night.
options.D: No, I don't need any more boxes.

items.explanation: 说话人看到对方独自搬一堆箱子，语气里带着关切和想帮忙的意思。正确答案B以'Here'开头，直接伸手接过几个箱子，用行动回应了帮忙的意图，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A项借用'boxes'一词，却谈箱子是再生纸板做的，属于语义联想陷阱。C项完全离题，说自己昨晚看了部好纪录片。D项把对方的关心误解成'要给我更多箱子'，回答'不用了'，属于误解型干扰项。

items.situation: asking a store clerk about a return or exchange
items.speaker: Can't I exchange this shirt for a bigger size?

options.A: That shirt comes in three other colors.
options.B: The parking meter only takes coins.
options.C: Sure, the bigger sizes are right over here.
options.D: We had a nice exchange about the weather.

items.explanation: 顾客问能不能把这件衬衫换成大一号的。正确答案C以话语标记'Sure'开头，答应可以换，并指出大码就在旁边，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A项借用'shirt'一词，却谈这件衬衫还有另外三种颜色，属于语义联想陷阱。B项完全离题，说停车计时器只收硬币。D项把'exchange'（换货）误当成'exchange'（交谈），说'我们聊了聊天气'，属于一词多义误解型干扰项。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's situation
items.speaker: Haven't you slept at all this week?

options.A: This mattress is great for deep sleep.
options.B: The bus was surprisingly empty today.
options.C: No, I haven't seen your keys anywhere.
options.D: I'm getting by, thanks for checking in.

items.explanation: 说话人担心地问朋友这周是不是根本没怎么睡。正确答案D使用口语习惯表达'getting by'（还撑得住）并加上'thanks for checking in'（谢谢你关心），语气温暖自然，符合idiomatic范式。A项借用'sleep'的联想，却谈床垫有助于深度睡眠，属于语义联想陷阱。B项完全离题，说今天公交车意外地空。C项把'Haven't you...'误解成在问有没有见过钥匙，答非所问，属于误解型干扰项。

items.situation: calling to check business hours
items.speaker: Are you open this Sunday morning?

options.A: Well, we don't open till noon Sundays.
options.B: Sunday brunch is our most popular menu.
options.C: The delivery truck comes on Tuesdays.
options.D: We're right across from the bank.

items.explanation: 打电话的人问周日上午是否营业。正确答案A以话语标记'Well'开头，间接告知周日要到中午才开门，既回应了是否营业，又给出了具体时间，符合marker_led_indirect范式。B项借用'Sunday'一词，却谈周日早午餐菜单最受欢迎，属于语义联想陷阱。C项完全离题，说送货车周二来。D项回答的是'店在哪里'（在银行对面）而非'是否/几点营业'，属于答错问题类型。

items.situation: student asking about gym or sports facility hours
items.speaker: I can't ever tell when the gym closes.

options.A: The gym just added new treadmills.
options.B: Actually, it stays open till midnight.
options.C: My roommate joined a pottery class.
options.D: The gym is close to the dining hall.

items.explanation: 说话人抱怨自己老搞不清健身房几点关门。正确答案B以话语标记'Actually'开头，间接给出解决办法——现在开到午夜，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A项借用'gym'一词，却谈健身房新添了跑步机，属于语义联想陷阱。C项完全离题，说自己室友报名了陶艺课。D项把'closes'（关门）误当成'close'（近），说健身房离食堂很近，属于一词多义误解型干扰项。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: What's the section with geology journals?

options.A: The geology final is next Tuesday.
options.B: The café upstairs closes at five.
options.C: Are you after print or digital?
options.D: The head librarian ordered them last spring.

items.explanation: 学生问图书馆员地质学期刊在哪个区。正确答案C用一个反问'你要纸质版还是电子版？'来推进对话、锁定需求，符合counter_question范式，是图书馆员自然的回应。A项借用'geology'一词，却谈地质学期末考试在下周二，属于语义联想陷阱。B项完全离题，说楼上咖啡馆五点关门。D项回答的是'谁/什么时候订的这些期刊'而非'在哪个区'，属于答错问题类型。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: How long's the final essay supposed to be?

options.A: The essay contest deadline passed already.
options.B: The third-floor printer is broken again.
options.C: It's due at the end of the month.
options.D: Well, aim for around ten pages.

items.explanation: 学生问教授期末论文应该写多长。正确答案D以话语标记'Well'开头，间接给出建议——大概写十页左右，符合marker_led_indirect范式。A项借用'essay'一词，却谈论文比赛截止日期已过，属于语义联想陷阱。B项完全离题，说三楼打印机又坏了。C项回答的是'什么时候交'（月底）而非'写多长'，属于答错问题类型。

items.situation: asking a store clerk about a return or exchange
items.speaker: Can't I return these boots without a receipt?

options.A: Yes, store credit works without one.
options.B: Those boots come in black too.
options.C: The mall closes early on holidays.
options.D: I'll return around three today.

items.explanation: 顾客问没有小票能不能退这双靴子。正确答案A直接给出事实回答——可以，没有小票会以购物积分形式退，符合direct_topical范式。B项借用'boots'一词，却谈这双靴子还有黑色，属于语义联想陷阱。C项完全离题，说商场节假日提早关门。D项把'return'（退货）误当成'return'（返回），说自己今天三点左右回来，属于一词多义误解型干扰项。

items.situation: student asking about a campus event or concert
items.speaker: Where's the jazz concert being held tonight?

options.A: The jazz band added three new members.
options.B: It's in the main auditorium.
options.C: I still owe on my meal plan.
options.D: Doors open at eight sharp.

items.explanation: 说话人问今晚爵士音乐会在哪里举办。正确答案B直接给出地点——在主礼堂，符合direct_topical范式。A项借用'jazz'一词，却谈爵士乐队新加了三名成员，属于语义联想陷阱。C项完全离题，说自己还欠着餐费。D项回答的是'什么时候开场'（八点准时开门）而非'在哪里'，属于答错问题类型。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: Why don't you just borrow my notes?

options.A: My notebook is almost totally full.
options.B: The vending machine ate my dollar.
options.C: You're a lifesaver, thanks so much!
options.D: Because I overslept this morning.

items.explanation: 同学主动提议'你何不借我的笔记'。正确答案C使用习惯表达'You're a lifesaver'（你真是救星）加上道谢，自然地接受了好意，符合idiomatic范式。A项借用'notes/notebook'的联想，却谈自己的笔记本快写满了，属于语义联想陷阱。B项完全离题，说自动售货机吞了自己一块钱。D项把这句提议当成字面质问，回答'因为我今早睡过头了'，答非所问且不合逻辑，属于答错问题类型。

items.situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed
items.speaker: You're really swamped with all this work.

options.A: These papers are stacked way too high.
options.B: The weather's finally warming up outside.
options.C: The swamp trail was muddy yesterday.
options.D: Honestly, I could use a hand.

items.explanation: 说话人看出对方被一堆活儿压得喘不过气，语气里带着想帮忙的意思。正确答案D以话语标记'Honestly'开头，使用习惯表达'could use a hand'（正需要人搭把手），坦然接受帮助，符合idiomatic范式。A项借用'work'的联想，却谈这些文件堆得太高，属于语义联想陷阱。B项完全离题，说外面天气终于回暖了。C项把'swamped'（忙得不可开交）误当成'swamp'（沼泽），说沼泽小径昨天很泥泞，属于一词多义误解型干扰项。

items.situation: friend asking another how they'll get to a study group
items.speaker: How're you getting to the study group tonight?

options.A: Don't worry, I'll drive us both.
options.B: The group finished the project last week.
options.C: It's supposed to rain all weekend.
options.D: Around seven, right after dinner.

items.explanation: A 正确：没有直接回答「怎么去」，而是解决背后的真实需求——直接提出开车带对方一起，属于 context_shift（转向解决问题）。B 错：抓住「study group」联想到小组做项目，是语义联想陷阱，答非所问。C 错：谈天气下雨，语法通顺但与本对话毫无关系，是离题干扰。D 错：说明「时间」是几点，但问题问的是「方式」，答错了问题类型。

items.situation: responding to an invitation to join others for dinner
items.speaker: Don't you want to join us for dinner?

options.A: That new restaurant is pretty pricey.
options.B: Count me in, absolutely!
options.C: She just moved into a new place.
options.D: I finally joined the gym yesterday.

items.explanation: B 正确：「Count me in」是习惯表达，意为「算我一个」，自然地接受了邀请，属于 idiomatic。A 错：抓住「dinner」联想到餐厅贵不贵，是语义联想陷阱，没有回应邀请。C 错：谈别人搬家，语法正确但完全离题。D 错：利用「join」的另一层含义（加入健身房 = 报名注册），是一词多义陷阱，误读了原句意图。

items.situation: student asking where the gym schedule is posted
items.speaker: Where's the gym schedule usually posted?

options.A: It opens at six every morning.
options.B: The gym just added new treadmills.
options.C: Look no further—it's on the door.
options.D: I forgot my water bottle again.

items.explanation: C 正确：「Look no further」是习惯表达，意为「不用再找了」，随后指出就在门上，自然又有用，属于 idiomatic。A 错：说明「几点开门」是时间信息，但问题问的是「在哪里张贴」，答错了问题类型。B 错：抓住「gym」联想到新添跑步机，是语义联想陷阱。D 错：说自己忘带水壶，语法通顺但离题。

items.situation: asking a neighbor why the shared laundry room is noisy
items.speaker: Why's the laundry room always so loud?

options.A: I ran out of detergent again.
options.B: The mail usually comes around noon.
options.C: It's right down the hall.
options.D: Have you tried the one upstairs?

items.explanation: D 正确：用一个反问推进对话——建议对方去楼上那间，间接回应了噪音问题，属于 counter_question。A 错：抓住「laundry」联想到洗衣液用完，是语义联想陷阱。B 错：谈收信时间，语法正确但完全离题。C 错：说明「位置」在走廊尽头，但问题问的是「为什么吵」，答错了问题类型。

items.situation: student seeking help with an essay at the writing center
items.speaker: What's the best way to fix my essay?

options.A: Well, let's start with your thesis.
options.B: The essay contest deadline is Friday.
options.C: The printers on this floor broke.
options.D: Yes, I'm free until noon.

items.explanation: A 正确：以话语标记「Well」开头，随后间接地从论点入手给出方向，属于 marker_led_indirect。B 错：抓住「essay」联想到征文比赛截止日期，是语义联想陷阱。C 错：谈打印机坏了，语法通顺但离题。D 错：用「是的，我到中午前有空」回答了「是否有空」这类是非问，但原句问的是「怎么修改」，答错了问题类型。

items.situation: student asking about the format of the final exam
items.speaker: Aren't the exam questions all multiple choice?

options.A: The exam hall is on the third floor.
options.B: Didn't the professor post a review sheet?
options.C: I left my calculator at home.
options.D: It's really your choice to make.

items.explanation: B 正确：用反问推进对话——提示对方教授贴过复习提纲，从那里能得到答案，属于 counter_question。A 错：抓住「exam」联想到考场在几楼，是语义联想陷阱。C 错：说自己把计算器落家里了，语法正确但离题。D 错：利用「choice」的另一层含义（选择/决定权，而非选择题），是一词多义陷阱。

items.situation: student unsure which books are on the course reading list
items.speaker: I'm not sure which books are on the reading list.

options.A: The reading room closes at nine.
options.B: My roommate borrowed my laptop again.
options.C: They're all listed on the syllabus.
options.D: You should read a bit slower.

items.explanation: C 正确：直接给出事实信息——书目都列在教学大纲上，属于 direct_topical。A 错：抓住「reading」联想到阅览室几点关门，是语义联想陷阱。B 错：谈室友借走笔记本电脑，语法通顺但离题。D 错：利用「read」的字面意思，把「reading list（书单）」误读成「读书速度」，是误解/一词多义陷阱。

items.situation: student checking the front desk for a lost umbrella
items.speaker: I think I've left my umbrella here somewhere.

options.A: It's been raining all week.
options.B: The cafeteria closes early on Fridays.
options.C: Yes, turn left past the hallway.
options.D: Actually, check that bin over there.

items.explanation: D 正确：以话语标记「Actually」开头，随后间接地指引对方去那个箱子找，属于 marker_led_indirect。A 错：抓住「umbrella」联想到下雨，是语义联想陷阱。B 错：谈食堂周五提前关门，语法正确但离题。C 错：利用「left」的另一层含义（方向「左」而非「落下」），是一词多义陷阱。

items.situation: roommate discussing weekend plans
items.speaker: Are you heading home for the long weekend?

options.A: Actually, I'm staying to catch up on work.
options.B: The weekend forecast looks pretty rainy.
options.C: My sister just started a new job.
options.D: By train, it's about three hours.

items.explanation: 室友问对方长周末是否回家。正确答案A以话语标记词"Actually"开头，间接地表明"不回，要留下来赶工作"，既回应了是否回家的问题，又自然地解释了原因，是真实对话的口吻。B是语义联想陷阱：抓住"weekend"联想到周末天气预报，却完全没回答回不回家。C是离题项：谈姐姐找到新工作，与是否回家毫无关联，只是另一场对话里成立的正常句子。D是答错问题类型："By train, it's about three hours"回答的是"怎么回去/多远"这类How问题，而对方问的是"要不要回家"这个是非问题，答非所问。只有A真正回应了行程决定。

items.situation: student asking about a reading list for a course
items.speaker: Why isn't the reading list posted online yet?

options.A: The reading room is on the second floor.
options.B: Beats me — ask the professor.
options.C: I finished the lab report last night.
options.D: Just post it on the bulletin board.

items.explanation: 学生问为什么课程阅读书单还没发到网上。正确答案B用地道口语"Beats me"（意为"我也不知道"）表达自己同样不清楚，并顺势建议去问教授，是对"为什么"这个问题最自然的间接回应。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住"reading"联想到"阅览室在二楼"，却和书单为何未发布无关。C是离题项：只说自己昨晚写完了实验报告，与书单毫无关系。D是词义误解陷阱：把"posted"（网上发布）误读成物理张贴，建议"贴到公告板上"，曲解了学生说的"online posted"的含义。只有B贴切地回应了这个Why问题。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: I'm looking for a book on Renaissance art.

options.A: That novel was made into a movie.
options.B: The café upstairs closes at six.
options.C: Well, I can look that up.
options.D: You can renew it online anytime.

items.explanation: 学生（对图书管理员）陈述自己想找一本关于文艺复兴艺术的书。正确答案C以话语标记词"Well"开头，间接地表示"我帮你查一下"，正是管理员面对这类请求最自然、最有帮助的回应。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住"book"联想到"那本小说被拍成了电影"，与帮忙找书无关。B是离题项：楼上咖啡馆六点关门，属于另一场对话，和找书毫无关系。D是词义误解陷阱："You can renew it online"假定学生已经借到了这本书、要续借，但学生只是刚开始找书，误读了当前情境。只有C真正推进了"帮你找到这本书"这件事。

items.situation: student asking about gym or sports facility hours
items.speaker: What time does the pool open on weekdays?

options.A: The pool was just repainted last month.
options.B: I forgot my towel again today.
options.C: It's next to the tennis courts.
options.D: How early were you hoping to swim?

items.explanation: 学生问泳池工作日几点开门。正确答案D以反问"你想多早去游泳？"回应，通过一个推进对话的问题来了解对方需求，是counter_question范式下地道的回应方式。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住"pool"联想到"泳池上个月刚重新粉刷"，却没回答开门时间。B是离题项：说自己又忘带毛巾，属于另一情境的正常句子，与开门时间无关。C是答错问题类型："It's next to the tennis courts"回答的是"在哪里"（Where），而对方问的是"几点"（What time），答非所问。只有D恰当地承接了关于开放时间的询问。

items.situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: The heater in my room's been broken all week.

options.A: Actually, maintenance is coming by this afternoon.
options.B: It's been unusually cold outside lately.
options.C: I really need to do my laundry.
options.D: Yes, I turned it off earlier.

items.explanation: 学生陈述宿舍暖气坏了一整周。正确答案A以话语标记词"Actually"开头，间接地告知"维修工今天下午就来"，直接回应了暖气问题背后的需求（尽快修好），是自然贴心的回应。B是语义联想陷阱：抓住"heater"相关的冷暖概念，联想到"最近外面特别冷"，却没解决暖气坏了的问题。C是离题项：说自己得去洗衣服，与暖气毫无关联。D是词义误解陷阱："Yes, I turned it off earlier"把暖气坏了误读成有人主动关掉了它，曲解了原意，且用"Yes"回应一句陈述句本就不搭。只有A真正回应并推进了暖气维修这件事。

items.situation: acknowledging someone's unavailability or schedule conflict
items.speaker: Aren't you tied up with rehearsals tonight?

options.A: The rehearsal hall is being renovated.
options.B: Don't worry, we can meet tomorrow instead.
options.C: My cousin is visiting from out of town.
options.D: Yes, I already tied up the boxes.

items.explanation: 说话人意识到对方今晚可能因排练而没空。正确答案B不纠结于"是/不是"，而是直接解决背后的问题——"别担心，我们改到明天见"，用context_shift的方式化解了时间冲突，是最得体的回应。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住"rehearsals"联想到"排练厅在装修"，却没处理时间安排。C是离题项：说表亲从外地来访，与今晚是否有空、如何安排无关。D是词义误解陷阱（一词多义）：把"tied up"（忙碌、抽不开身）误当成字面意思"捆扎"，回答"我已经把箱子捆好了"，完全曲解。只有B真正回应并解决了双方的时间冲突。

items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: Where's all that hammering coming from?

options.A: I just bought a new set of tools.
options.B: The recycling bins were moved outside.
options.C: Your guess is as good as mine.
options.D: It started around eight this morning.

items.explanation: 邻居问那阵敲打声是从哪儿传来的。正确答案C用地道习语"Your guess is as good as mine"（意为"我也不知道，跟你一样猜不到"）自然地表示自己同样不清楚声音来源，是idiomatic范式下贴切的回应。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住"hammering"联想到"我刚买了一套新工具"，却没说声音从哪来。B是离题项：谈回收桶被挪到了外面，与敲打声来源无关。D是答错问题类型："It started around eight this morning"回答的是"什么时候"（When），而对方问的是"从哪里"（Where），答非所问。只有C恰当地回应了这个Where问题。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: How do I sign up for your office hours?

options.A: My office is being repainted this week.
options.B: The department picnic is next Friday.
options.C: They're on Tuesdays and Thursdays.
options.D: Just use the sign-up sheet on my door.

items.explanation: 学生问怎样预约教授的答疑时间。正确答案D直接给出方法——"用我门上的登记表"，是direct_topical范式下对"How"问题最清楚的正面回答。A是语义联想陷阱：抓住"office"联想到"我的办公室这周在重新粉刷"，却没告诉学生怎么预约。B是离题项：说院系野餐在下周五，与预约方式无关。C是答错问题类型："They're on Tuesdays and Thursdays"回答的是答疑时间在"什么时候"（When），而学生问的是"怎样报名"（How），并没有告诉预约的方法。只有D直接解答了如何登记预约。
